map.area: Frontend Router and App Shell (PecanRev E2E Test Foundation)
map.summary: PecanRev frontend uses React Router (BrowserRouter) with a top-level route table in App.jsx (src/App.jsx). The router supports two parallel UI modes: legacy (classic) and Stitch (admin-only enterprise design). The Stitch shell (StitchAppShell) wraps the workspace with a 72px purple global rail, optional 280px contextual white submenu, and a slim top header. All routes below /app/project/:id use a unified workspace shell regardless of workflow stage (screening, extraction, RoB, analysis, etc.). Authentication is enforced by three wrappers: ProtectedRoute (signed-in), PublicRoute (unsigned-out), AdminRoute (admin+mod only, 404-cloaked). Deep-link query params (?tab=, ?view=, ?screen=, ?ui=) preserve navigation state across reloads. Feature flags gated on the backend control whether each engine is available (aiScreening, rob_engine_v2, networkMetaAnalysis, searchEngine, etc.).

routes.notes: Public landing page. BetaWaitlistGate shows beta waitlist page if betaWaitlist flag is ON to unauthenticated visitors; signed-in users see Landing as normal. No data-testid on Landing component itself.
Beta waitlist preview (noindex). Renders regardless of flag so admins can preview. preview prop=true.
Public ToS + Privacy Policy. Works signed in or out.
Redirect to /terms#privacy.
Auth page. PublicRoute redirects signed-in users to /app. Query param ?invite=<token> persists across login/register.
Auth page. PublicRoute redirects signed-in users to /app. Query param ?invite=<token> persists.
Invite landing. Unwrapped: works signed-in AND signed-out. No auth guard (deliberately).
Password reset. Unwrapped: works signed-out AND signed-in. /reset = request link; /reset?token=… = choose new password.
Email verification landing (public). Only rendered when requireEmailVerification is enabled.
Dynamic server-driven onboarding (prompt32). Post-registration or when admin adds questions. OnboardingGate exempt path.

Protected post-login home (dashboard). Query param ?view= selects dashboard tab (overview|mywork|invitations|archived|resources). DesignRoute renders StitchDashboard for admins with Stitch mode ON, else legacy ProjectLanding.

Protected workspace. Opens one project by id. Query param ?tab=<stage> selects workflow stage (overview|control|pico|prospero|search|screening|extraction|rob|analysis|forest|sensitivity|subgroup|nma|grade|manuscript|report|methods). ?screen=<key> deep-links screening subnav (import|duplicates|screening|conflicts|second-review|control|export). ?ui=legacy forces legacy UI even for admins in Stitch mode. All stages now render inside unified Stitch workspace (no standalone /sift-beta or /rob escapes).
Protected user profile. DesignRoute renders StitchProfile for admins in Stitch, else legacy Profile.

Internal admin console (staff only). LEGACY ONLY: never rendered in Stitch. ForceLegacyDesign pins this route to legacy even for admins whose global preference is Stitch (Stitch ops surface removed). AdminRoute 404-cloaks for non-staff.

Screening dashboard (staff only). Direct /sift-beta routes are STAFF-ONLY (AdminRoute = admin|mod, 404-cloaked). Regular users access screening through integrated project workspace (/app/project/:id?tab=screening), which server-enforces project access on every screening API.
Screening project (staff only). Direct route for staff; regular users use /app/project/:id?tab=screening.
Screening import (staff only). Direct route for staff workflow.
Risk-of-Bias workspace (gated on rob_engine_v2 flag). Renders as /app/project/:id?tab=rob inside unified workspace.
Risk-of-Bias workspace for a specific project. Renders as /app/project/:id?tab=rob inside unified workspace.
Fallback 404 handler. Redirects unknown paths to home (/).

uiElements.name: Global Rail Navigation Items (Dashboard, Activity, Invitations, Help)
StitchProjectRail (Project Workflow Rail - Purple Category Rail)
Workflow Step Rows (in Project Rail - numbered pips with connectors)
Workflow Stepper Step Row (numbered step in submenu)

behaviors.name: Admin Switches to Stitch UI
behaviors.description: An admin user (role=admin) toggles the design mode and the entire app switches from legacy to Stitch presentation. DesignRoute renders the Stitch component; html[data-ui-design='stitch'] attribute appears.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Log in as admin. 2. Locate AdminDesignSwitch (floating overlay). 3. Click to toggle to Stitch. 4. Assert html[data-ui-design='stitch'] is present. 5. Assert StitchAppShell renders (purple rail visible). 6. Toggle back to legacy; assert html[data-ui-design] is removed or set to 'legacy'.

behaviors.name: Non-Admin Cannot Access Stitch UI
behaviors.description: A non-admin user sees only the legacy UI regardless of any stored design preference. DesignRoute always renders legacy component.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Log in as non-admin user. 2. Navigate to /app. 3. Assert only legacy ProjectLanding renders. 4. Assert no purple rail visible. 5. Assert AdminDesignSwitch is not rendered.

behaviors.name: Global Rail Navigation Updates Active State
behaviors.description: Clicking a global rail button (Dashboard, Activity, Invitations, Help) updates aria-current='page' on that button and navigates to the correct route (/app or /app?view=). Other buttons lose aria-current.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Render StitchDashboard. 2. Click 'Activity' button in global rail. 3. Assert Activity button has aria-current='page'. 4. Assert route changed to /app?view=activity. 5. Assert Dashboard button no longer has aria-current='page'. 6. Repeat for other global nav items.

behaviors.name: Project Rail Expands on Hover (Desktop)
behaviors.description: On desktop (≥1024px), the project rail is 72px collapsed by default. On hover or keyboard focus, it expands to 248px (overlay, content does not reflow). On pin click, it becomes expanded-pinned (content reflows).
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Navigate to /app/project/:id in Stitch at ≥1024px viewport. 2. Assert rail width is 72px (getComputedStyle(rail, 'width')). 3. Hover over rail. 4. Assert rail width is 248px. 5. Unhover. 6. Assert rail width returns to 72px. 7. Click pin button. 8. Assert pinned state persists (data-pinned='true'). 9. Assert main content shifts to accommodate wider rail.

behaviors.name: Mobile Drawer Opens/Closes
behaviors.description: On mobile (<1024px), the hamburger in the top header opens an off-canvas drawer with the rail and context submenu stacked vertically. Clicking hamburger or pressing Escape closes it.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Resize to <1024px. 2. Assert mobile-only hamburger button is visible. 3. Click hamburger. 4. Assert StitchDrawer is open (aria-hidden='false' or visible). 5. Assert rail and context submenu stack vertically inside drawer. 6. Click outside drawer or press Escape. 7. Assert drawer closes.

behaviors.name: Project Stage Navigation via Rail
behaviors.description: Clicking a workflow step (Plan, Protocol, Search, Screen, Extract, etc.) in the project rail navigates to /app/project/:id?tab=<stage> and updates the workspace content. The step button shows aria-current='step'.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Render StitchProjectWorkspace for a project. 2. Click 'Screen' step in rail. 3. Assert route changed to ?tab=screening. 4. Assert Screen button has aria-current='step'. 5. Assert workspace content shows screening UI (SiftProject embedded). 6. Click 'Extract' step. 7. Assert route changed to ?tab=extraction and content updates.

behaviors.name: Screening Subnav Appears When Tab=Screening
behaviors.description: When ?tab=screening is active, StitchProjectSubnav renders in the context rail with screening subnav items (Overview, Import, Duplicates, Screening, Conflicts, Final Review, Settings, Export). Clicking a subitem deep-links to ?tab=screening&screen=<key>.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Navigate to /app/project/:id?tab=screening. 2. Assert StitchProjectSubnav renders in context rail. 3. Assert screening subnav buttons visible (Overview, Import, etc.). 4. Click 'Import'. 5. Assert route changed to ?tab=screening&screen=import. 6. Assert SiftProject reads ?screen= param and renders import view.

behaviors.name: Workflow Stepper Shows Correct Step Status
behaviors.description: The stepper in the project rail displays steps with correct status colors and glyphs (done=green check, partial=yellow, attention=red alert, empty=muted number). Connector lines above completed steps are green.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Render StitchProjectWorkspace. 2. Inspect stepper pips for expected colors (--t-success for done steps, etc.). 3. Assert connector line above a 'done' step is green (salpha(S.success, 0.5)). 4. Assert connector line above incomplete step is muted (rgba(255,255,255,0.16)). 5. Assert aria-label announces status ('done', 'needs attention', etc.).

behaviors.name: Disabled Workflow Steps Show Lock Icon
behaviors.description: Steps that are not yet available (no href) appear disabled with cursor:not-allowed, opacity 0.6, and a lock affordance. The aria-label includes 'Available once screening is set up'.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Render StitchProjectWorkspace for a new project (no screening). 2. Inspect Extract, Analysis, etc. steps. 3. Assert disabled={true}. 4. Assert aria-disabled=true. 5. Assert cursor is not-allowed on hover. 6. Assert lock icon is visible. 7. Assert aria-label includes disabled hint.

behaviors.name: Profile Button Opens Account Menu
behaviors.description: Clicking the profile avatar button in the primary rail opens StitchAccountMenu dropdown with profile, theme toggle, Ops Console (for staff), and sign out options.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Click profile avatar button in global rail. 2. Assert StitchAccountMenu renders (role='menu'). 3. Assert menu items visible: profile, theme toggle, sign out. 4. If admin: assert 'Ops Console' option visible. 5. Click 'Sign out'. 6. Assert navigates to /login or session clears.

behaviors.name: Theme Toggle Updates Display
behaviors.description: Clicking 'Theme' in account menu toggles between light and dark themes. CSS variables (--t-bg, --t-text, etc.) update and persist to localStorage.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Open account menu. 2. Inspect current theme (light/dark). 3. Click 'Theme' item. 4. Assert CSS variables change (getComputedStyle(document.documentElement, '--t-bg')). 5. Reload page. 6. Assert theme persists (localStorage check or visual inspection).

behaviors.name: Back to Projects Link Navigates to Dashboard
behaviors.description: Clicking 'Back to Projects' in the project rail navigates directly to /app (dashboard), not history.back().
behaviors.howToTest: 1. From /app/project/:id?tab=screening, click 'Back to Projects'. 2. Assert navigates to /app. 3. Assert route is exactly /app (not a history pop).

behaviors.name: Onboarding Gate Redirects Pending Users
behaviors.description: When a user has pendingOnboarding items, OnboardingGate redirects to /onboarding (except on exempt paths like /onboarding, /invite, /verify-email, /terms, /reset).
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Mock useAuth to return pendingOnboarding=[{...}]. 2. Navigate to /app/project/:id. 3. Assert redirects to /onboarding. 4. Complete onboarding. 5. Navigate to /app/project/:id again. 6. Assert no redirect (stays on page).

behaviors.name: ProtectedRoute Blocks Unauthenticated Access
behaviors.description: Navigating to /app, /app/project/:id, /profile, or /onboarding while unauthenticated redirects to /login. Shows LoadingScreen while auth check is in flight.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Clear auth session. 2. Navigate to /app. 3. Assert shows LoadingScreen while loading=true. 4. Once auth resolves (user=null), assert redirects to /login. 5. Assert no flash of protected content.

behaviors.name: PublicRoute Redirects Authenticated Users
behaviors.description: Navigating to /login or /register while authenticated redirects to /app. Shows nothing (null) while auth check is in flight.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Log in as user. 2. Navigate to /login. 3. Assert redirects to /app immediately. 4. No flash of login form. 5. Repeat for /register.

behaviors.name: AdminRoute 404-Cloaks Non-Staff
behaviors.description: Navigating to /ops, /sift-beta routes as non-staff (or unauthenticated) renders a generic 404 page, not an 'Access Denied' message.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Log in as non-admin user. 2. Navigate to /ops. 3. Assert renders generic 404 (large '404' text). 4. Assert no 'Access Denied' or 'Unauthorized' message. 5. Repeat for /sift-beta. 6. As admin, navigate to same routes. 7. Assert actual content renders (not 404).

behaviors.name: Deep-Link Query Params Preserve State
behaviors.description: Reloading a deep-linked URL (e.g., /app/project/:id?tab=screening&screen=import) restores the exact same view without losing query params.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Navigate to /app/project/:id?tab=screening&screen=import. 2. Assert SiftProject shows import view. 3. Reload page (F5). 4. Assert URL is unchanged. 5. Assert import view is restored (no need to re-navigate).

behaviors.name: Design Mode Does Not Leak Between Routes
behaviors.description: Switching design mode on /app does not affect /ops (which is always legacy via ForceLegacyDesign). Navigating to /ops shows legacy, then back to /app shows Stitch if previously selected.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Switch to Stitch mode on /app. 2. Navigate to /ops. 3. Assert /ops renders in legacy (ForceLegacyDesign). 4. Navigate back to /app. 5. Assert Stitch mode is still active.

behaviors.name: Feature Flags Gate Engine Availability
behaviors.description: Workflow stages (screening, rob_engine_v2, networkMetaAnalysis, searchEngine, etc.) are gated behind feature flags. If a flag is OFF, the step may be hidden or disabled in the rail.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Mock feature flag rob_engine_v2=false. 2. Render StitchProjectRail. 3. Assert RoB step is not present or disabled. 4. Set rob_engine_v2=true. 5. Assert RoB step is present and enabled.

behaviors.name: GlobalPresence Hook Pings Presence Endpoint
behaviors.description: The GlobalPresence component heartbeats /api/presence/ping whenever a signed-in user is on any route, so they show online in the Ops console even outside projects.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Log in as user. 2. Navigate to /app (dashboard). 3. Intercept network requests. 4. Assert /api/presence/ping is called periodically (e.g., every 10s). 5. Sign out. 6. Assert ping stops.

behaviors.name: Context Rail Collapse Button Works (Desktop)
behaviors.description: In the context rail header, the collapse button (onCollapse prop) allows users to hide the submenu. On desktop, this shrinks the layout; on mobile in drawer, context is always shown.
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Render StitchProjectWorkspace at ≥1024px with context rail visible. 2. Click collapse button in context rail header. 3. Assert context rail is hidden. 4. Assert main content expands. 5. Click expand (if available). 6. Assert context rail reappears.

behaviors.name: Responsive CSS Media Queries Hide Stitch on Legacy Browsers
behaviors.description: CSS media queries ensure <1024px shows only mobile drawer (no side-by-side rails), ≥1024px shows desktop layout. Stitch-scoped styles are conditional on html[data-ui-design='stitch'].
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Resize to <1024px. 2. Assert .stitch-desktop-nav has display:none. 3. Assert .stitch-mobile-only is visible. 4. Resize to ≥1024px. 5. Assert .stitch-desktop-nav is visible. 6. Assert .stitch-mobile-only is hidden.

seeding.what: Admin user with Stitch UI preference
seeding.how: 1. Create/log in as admin (role='admin'). 2. Save design mode preference to localStorage or mock useDesignMode().isStitch=true. 3. Navigate to /app.

1. Call POST /api/projects with title, description. 2. Call POST /api/projects/:id/screening to link a Sift screening workspace. 3. Call POST /api/projects/:id/screening/import to add articles. 4. Project now shows 'Screen' step enabled in rail.
1. Create project. 2. Set project._completedPhases = ['plan', 'protocol', 'search'] via API or direct data injection. 3. Workflow stepper shows green connectors and checkmarks for those steps.
1. Invite user via POST /api/projects/:id/invitations. 2. Mocked useInvitations() returns pendingCount > 0. 3. Invitations button in global rail shows badge.
1. Mock useAuth to return pendingOnboarding=[{ id: 'q1', question: '...' }]. 2. Navigate to any protected route. 3. OnboardingGate redirects to /onboarding.
1. Mock feature flag getter: features.rob_engine_v2 = true. 2. RoB workflow step becomes available in project rail.

featureFlags.howToEnable: Set server feature flag aiScreening=true. AI-powered screening engines appear in screening UI.
Set server feature flag rob_engine_v2=true. RoB workflow step becomes available; /rob route and ?tab=rob become functional.
Set server feature flag networkMetaAnalysis=true. Network Meta-Analysis step appears in workflow; NMA tab mounts.
Set server feature flag searchEngine=true. Unified search wizard (Define → Build → Run) replaces legacy Search/Discovery tabs.
Set server feature flag pecanSearch=true. PecanSearch-specific search behavior activates.
Set server feature flag betaWaitlist=true. Unauthenticated visitors see beta waitlist landing instead of main Landing.
Set server feature flag serverBackedWorkflowState=true. Workflow state syncs from server instead of local Redux store.

gaps: No data-testid attributes exist on most Stitch shell components. Recommend adding: data-testid='stitch-app-shell', data-testid='stitch-primary-rail', data-testid='stitch-context-rail', data-testid='stitch-workflow-stepper-step-{num}', data-testid='stitch-project-rail', etc.

RailButton and StepRow components use inline styles exclusively; no CSS classes for stable selectors. Consider adding a className prop (e.g., className='stitch-rail-button') or wrapping in a div with a data-testid.
The screening subnav deep-link (?tab=screening&screen=<key>) is handled inside SiftProject component, not at the router level. Tests must verify SiftProject reads ?screen= param correctly.
Admin design switch (AdminDesignSwitch) is not permanently visible; it floats in the top-right corner. Tests must find it within the viewport or mock its state directly.
Feature flags are fetched from the server (not localStorage). No offline/client-side way to toggle them in tests without mocking the API.

The 'pin' state for the project rail (useSidebarPin hook) persists to localStorage, but the hook implementation is not visible in provided files. Tests should mock this hook or verify localStorage['stitch-sidebar-pinned'].
useProjectPresence and useScreeningSummary are real data hooks that depend on API calls. Tests should mock these or use a seeded project with real data.
Onboarding gate uses location.pathname matching; deep-link test should verify exact path matching logic (e.g., '/onboarding' matches, but '/onboarding/step2' may not).
The GlobalPresence component renders null and is not visible in the DOM; its effect is entirely network-based. Tests verify via network interception, not DOM assertions.
StitchErrorBoundary exists but its implementation file is not provided; tests cannot verify error recovery without reading that file.
The nested lazy-import structure (LazyControl, LazyScreening, etc.) means each tab is code-split. Tests should verify bundle sizes and chunk loading, not just content rendering.

map.area: AUTH & ONBOARDING: Landing, Login, Register, Email Verification, Onboarding Questions, Profile/Account, Logout, Terms
map.summary: The AUTH & ONBOARDING area flows through a public landing page (Landing.jsx), unauthenticated login/register pages (Login.jsx, Register.jsx), email verification when enabled (VerifyEmail.jsx), server-driven onboarding questions (Onboarding.jsx), profile management (Profile.jsx), and terms/privacy (Terms.jsx). Session state is managed by AuthContext.jsx which checks GET /api/auth/me on app startup; protected routes use ProtectedRoute, PublicRoute, and AdminRoute guards. Onboarding is server-driven via /api/onboarding/pending and supports per-question skip logic. The UserMenu.jsx dropdown provides account access and logout from anywhere in the app.

routes.notes: Public home page; BetaWaitlistGate wraps it to show beta waitlist page when flag ON. For unauthenticated visitors only—signed-in users see same page.
PublicRoute wraps it (redirects authenticated users to /app). Supports ?invite=<token> query param for invite handoff.
PublicRoute wraps it. Supports ?invite=<token> for invite flow. Auto-accepts invites on registration.
Public email verification landing. Reads ?token= from query string. Only used when requireEmailVerification flag is ON.
Protected route (ProtectedRoute wrapper). Server-driven onboarding questions fetched from GET /api/onboarding/pending. OnboardingGate exempt path (never redirected away).

routes.component: Profile (lazy) → wrapped in ProtectedRoute + OnboardingGate + DesignRoute
routes.notes: Protected account management page. Renders legacy Profile or Stitch StitchProfile via DesignRoute.
Public Terms of Service + Privacy Policy. Supports anchor links #terms and #privacy.
Convenience redirect to the privacy policy anchor on Terms page.
Public password reset flow. Works signed-in and signed-out. Two modes: /reset (request link), /reset?token=… (choose new password).
Invite landing. Deliberately unwrapped (must work signed-in AND signed-out). Can redirect to project on accept.
Admin-only preview route for beta waitlist page (noindex). Renders regardless of betaWaitlist flag.

featureFlags.howToEnable: Admin enables via /ops console (Settings or equivalent). When ON, registration redirects to /verify-email instead of /app or /onboarding.
Admin enables flag. When ON, unauthenticated visitors see the BetaWaitlistPage instead of Landing; authenticated users and /beta-waitlist preview route bypass it.

uiElements.name: VerifyEmail Go to Sign In Button
VerifyEmail Back to Sign In Link

behaviors.name: Login Success Flow
behaviors.description: User enters valid email/password, submits form. AuthClient.login() → /api/auth/login succeeds, AuthContext.login() called, user redirected to /app (or /onboarding if pending questions, or /invite/:token if invite present).
behaviors.howToTest: Fill login form with valid credentials (from seeding), click Sign in, wait for navigate to /app or /onboarding. Assert AuthContext.user is set. Check session cookie exists (credentials: include).

behaviors.name: Login Failure - Invalid Credentials
behaviors.description: User enters wrong email or password. AuthClient.login() throws error with message from server ({error: 'Invalid email or password'}). Error banner renders with message.
behaviors.howToTest: Enter invalid email/password, submit. Verify error message appears in red banner at L226. Field values should persist. Submit button should not be disabled after error.

behaviors.name: Login Failure - Missing Fields
behaviors.description: Email or password field is empty. HTML5 required validation prevents submission; browser shows native validation message.
behaviors.howToTest: Leave email or password empty, attempt submit. Verify browser validation message appears. Check HTML5 required attributes on inputs.

behaviors.name: Register Success Flow
behaviors.description: User fills name, email, password, confirm, terms checkbox. AuthClient.register() → /api/auth/register succeeds. On success: if inviteToken present, auto-accepts invite via POST /api/invites/:token/accept; if requireEmailVerification ON, redirect to /verify-email; else redirect to /onboarding or /app.
behaviors.howToTest: Fill register form with new user data, check terms, submit. Trace redirect based on feature flags. Verify session cookie set. Check pendingOnboarding populated or email verification page reached.

behaviors.name: Register Failure - Password Mismatch
behaviors.description: Password and confirm password fields differ. Client-side validation triggers error banner: 'Passwords do not match.'
behaviors.howToTest: Enter different values in password and confirm fields, submit. Verify error banner with exact message appears. Form should not submit.

behaviors.name: Register Failure - Password Too Short
behaviors.description: Password length < 8 chars. Client-side validation shows error: 'Password must be at least 8 characters.'
behaviors.howToTest: Enter password with 7 or fewer chars, submit. Verify error message. Ensure no server round-trip occurs.

behaviors.name: Register Failure - Invalid Email
behaviors.description: Email does not match /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/ (client-side EMAIL_RE). Error: 'Enter a valid email address (e.g. you@institution.edu).'
behaviors.howToTest: Enter invalid emails (missing @, missing TLD), submit. Verify client-side error. No server round-trip should occur.

behaviors.name: Register Failure - Terms Not Agreed
behaviors.description: Terms checkbox unchecked. Client-side validation shows error: 'Please agree to the Terms and Privacy Policy to continue.'
behaviors.howToTest: Leave terms checkbox unchecked, fill other fields, submit. Verify error message. Uncheck after form fill should cause error on next submit.

behaviors.name: Register Invite Handoff - Success
behaviors.description: Register with ?invite=<token>. After registration succeeds, client auto-POSTs /api/invites/:token/accept (idempotent). On accept success, redirects to /sift-beta/projects/:projectId instead of /app.
behaviors.howToTest: Generate valid invite token (via seeding), register via /register?invite=<token>. Verify auto-accept network request fires. Check redirect to project workspace, not /app.

behaviors.name: Register Invite Handoff - Fallback
behaviors.description: If invite accept fails (e.g., already claimed, expired), fallback to /invite/:token page where user can retry or see error.
behaviors.howToTest: Register with expired/invalid invite. Verify redirect to /invite/:token (fallback). Auto-accept error should not hard-block registration.

behaviors.name: Email Verification - Success
behaviors.description: User navigates to /verify-email?token=<token>. VerifyEmail.jsx POSTs /api/auth/verify-email with token. On success, status = 'ok', message = 'Your email is verified...', 'Go to sign in' button appears.
behaviors.howToTest: Generate valid verification token, navigate to /verify-email?token=<token>. Wait for verification. Verify status text changes and button appears. Click to go to /login.

behaviors.name: Email Verification - Invalid Token
behaviors.description: Token is missing, invalid, or expired. POST /api/auth/verify-email fails. Status = 'error', message = 'This verification link is invalid or has expired.' Resend link button appears.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to /verify-email without token, or with bad token. Verify error state and resend button. Click resend, enter email in prompt, verify POST /api/auth/resend-verification fires.

behaviors.name: Email Verification - Resend
behaviors.description: User clicks 'Send a new link', enters email via window.prompt(). Client POSTs /api/auth/resend-verification. No-enumeration policy: always resolves OK (whether email exists or not). Button disabled, message 'Link sent (check your inbox)' shown.
behaviors.howToTest: On verification failed page, click resend. Verify prompt appears. Enter email, verify POST to /api/auth/resend-verification. Button should disable and show success message.

behaviors.name: Onboarding - Fetch Pending
behaviors.description: On mount, Onboarding.jsx calls getPendingOnboarding(). GET /api/onboarding/pending (requireAuth) returns {questions: [...], intro: {...}}. Progress bar renders with question count.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to /onboarding after login. Wait for questions to load. Verify loading state briefly, then questions rendered. Count displayed as '1 / N'.

behaviors.name: Onboarding - No Pending Questions
behaviors.description: GET /api/onboarding/pending returns empty questions array. Page immediately redirects to /app (nothing to ask).
behaviors.howToTest: Login as user with no pending questions. Navigate to /onboarding. Should immediately redirect to /app. No loading flicker should be visible.

behaviors.name: Onboarding - Answer Required Question
behaviors.description: Question has isRequired=true. User fills field (text, select, date, etc). Clicking Continue validates answer (isAnswered()). If empty, shows error: 'This question is required...' If filled, POSTs /api/onboarding/responses with answer, moves to next question or completes.
behaviors.howToTest: On onboarding page, fill required text field, click Continue. Verify answer submitted via POST. Progress bar advances. Try skipping without answer—verify error banner.

behaviors.name: Onboarding - Skip Skippable Question
behaviors.description: Question has isRequired=false and allowSkip !== false. Skip button visible. Click Skip → POSTs /api/onboarding/skip with [questionId]. Moves to next question or redirects to /app.
behaviors.howToTest: On a skippable question (verify allowSkip property), click Skip. Verify POST to /api/onboarding/skip. Progress bar advances. No answer should be saved.

behaviors.name: Onboarding - Skip All
behaviors.description: If all remaining questions are skippable, 'Skip all' button appears. Click → POSTs /api/onboarding/skip (no questionIds). Marks all pending as skipped, redirects to /app.
behaviors.howToTest: Reach onboarding with all skippable questions. Verify 'Skip all' button. Click. Verify POST /api/onboarding/skip (no body). Redirect to /app immediately.

behaviors.name: Onboarding - Network Failure Fallback
behaviors.description: If GET /api/onboarding/pending or any POST fails, the page never hard-blocks. Errors are swallowed, user advanced anyway or redirected to /app.
behaviors.howToTest: Simulate network failure on onboarding endpoints (via DevTools throttle or mock fail). Verify page does not show error screen, user can still navigate to /app.

behaviors.name: Onboarding - Server-Driven Gate on Protected Routes
behaviors.description: OnboardingGate checks pendingOnboarding from AuthContext on every protected route. If length > 0 and route not exempt, redirects to /onboarding.
behaviors.howToTest: Login, trigger new pending questions (admin adds question, or re-fetch on existing user). Navigate to /app. Verify OnboardingGate redirects to /onboarding automatically.

behaviors.name: Profile - Load Account Info

behaviors.name: Profile - Edit Name
behaviors.description: User edits name field in Profile section. Click Save. POSTs /api/profile/{endpoint} with updated name. Verify response updates local state and shows success feedback.

behaviors.name: Profile - Change Password
behaviors.description: User enters current password and new password (with confirm). Click Change Password. Validates, POSTs /api/profile/change-password. On success, clears fields and shows 'Password changed' toast.

behaviors.name: Session Persistence - Startup Check
behaviors.description: App launches. AuthProvider useEffect calls getMe(). GET /api/auth/me (requires httpOnly session cookie). If 401, returns null (unauthenticated). If 200, returns user object.
behaviors.howToTest: Load app with valid session cookie. Verify network request to /api/auth/me. Check AuthContext.user populated, loading → false. ProtectedRoute allows access.

behaviors.name: Session Persistence - Unauthenticated Startup
behaviors.description: App launches without valid session. getMe() returns null. AuthContext.loading → false, AuthContext.user = null. ProtectedRoute redirects to /login.
behaviors.howToTest: Clear cookies, reload app. Verify /api/auth/me fires and gets 401. Public routes render, protected routes show login redirect.

behaviors.name: Logout Flow
behaviors.description: User clicks 'Sign out' in UserMenu. handleLogout() calls logout(). Client POSTs /api/auth/logout (requires auth). AuthContext clears user + pendingOnboarding. Navigate to '/'.
behaviors.howToTest: Authenticated user clicks UserMenu, then Sign out. Verify POST /api/auth/logout fires. Check session cookie cleared (httpOnly, so invisible in JS but server should drop it). User redirected to landing page.

behaviors.name: Login/Register Redirect When Authenticated
behaviors.description: Authenticated user navigates to /login or /register. PublicRoute checks useAuth(). If user exists, redirects to /app.
behaviors.howToTest: Login, then navigate to /login. Verify immediate redirect to /app (or /onboarding if pending). No login form should render.

behaviors.name: Protected Route Loading State
behaviors.description: ProtectedRoute shows loading screen (⬡ spinner + 'Loading...') while AuthContext.loading = true. Once auth check completes, loading → false and content renders.
behaviors.howToTest: Add network throttle to slow auth check. Navigate to protected route. Observe loading screen briefly. Once auth completes, content renders.

behaviors.name: AdminRoute Existence Hiding
behaviors.description: Non-admin user navigates to /ops or other AdminRoute. STAFF_ROLES=['admin','mod'] check fails. GenericNotFound (404 page) renders instead of 'Access Denied'. Route existence is not revealed.
behaviors.howToTest: Login as regular user. Navigate to /ops. Verify 404 page, not access-denied. No error message should reveal the route exists.

behaviors.name: Terms Page Anchor Links
behaviors.description: Navigate to /terms#terms or /terms#privacy. useEffect scrolls to element with matching id. Anchors preserved from public links.
behaviors.howToTest: Click Terms link from Register page or navigate to /terms#privacy directly. Verify page scrolls to privacy section. Back button works.

seeding.how: Run server/scripts/seedAdmins.js or direct DB insert: User {email, password (hashed), role='admin', ...}. Admin can access /ops, enable feature flags, add onboarding questions.
POST /api/auth/register with new email + password. Or direct DB insert: User {email, password (hashed), role='user', onboardingCompleted=true}.
Admin adds new OnboardingQuestion {isRequired=true/false, ...}. On next login/fetch, GET /api/onboarding/pending returns it. User redirected to /onboarding.
POST /api/auth/register, then direct DB update: User {emailVerified=true}. Or user receives email link, navigates to /verify-email?token=<token>, POSTs /api/auth/verify-email.
Enable requireEmailVerification flag (admin setting). POST /api/auth/register triggers email send. User redirected to /verify-email on registration success.
Admin creates invite via POST /api/invites or direct DB insert: Invite {token, projectId, expiresAt, ...}. User can register via /register?invite=<token> and auto-join project.
POST /api/auth/login or /api/auth/register sets Set-Cookie: session=<jwt>. Browser auto-includes credentials: include in subsequent requests. Cannot be read via JS.

map.area: Dashboard and Project Lifecycle (Stitch Admin UI)
map.summary: The PecanRev Stitch dashboard and project lifecycle is centralized in StitchDashboard.jsx (/app route with ?view= query param for tabs) and spans multiple views: overview (with KPI cards, project list, filters), mywork, activity, invitations, archived, and resources. Project creation/deletion/rename flows are modal-based with confirmation requirements. The StitchProjectOverview page (/app/project/:projectId) displays a single project's status, members, and provides navigation to workflow stages. All modals are portal-rendered overlays with focus trapping, and project cards show inline action buttons for open/rename/archive/delete. Routing is unified: every workflow stage opens as a tab within StitchProjectWorkspace (not separate engine routes). State is fetched from api.projects.* endpoints; real-time updates come via useRealtime() hooks listening to server events (project.updated, members.changed, etc.). Long project names truncate with ellipsis (overflow hidden); deletion requires exact name match confirmation with leading/trailing whitespace trimmed.

routes.notes: Main dashboard hub. ?view=overview (default) shows KPI cards + project list. Other views: mywork, activity, invitations, archived, resources. Each view is a real data-backed surface, not placeholder.

Workspace overview: 4 KPI metric cards (Active projects, Owned by you, Studies imported, Screening records), 2-column bento (Your progress ring + Recently updated list), then project list with filters (All/Active/In progress/Completed/Owned by me) and search.
Work needing user attention across projects (role-aware, from overviewModel.js buildMyWork).
Per-user notification stream (merged Recent Activity).
Project invitations & collaboration. Badge shows pending count (from useInvitations hook).
Archived projects list. Same grid layout as active project list.
Help, documentation & feedback links.

Project command center. Shows compact header, status badges, workflow phases (Plan/Search/Screen/Extract/Analyze/Report), progress ring, attention items, my work, metrics, and team roster. No ?tab= means overview; appending ?tab=<stageName> opens that workflow stage.
Explicit overview tab (same as bare route).
Plan phase, PICO workflow stage. Routes to overviewTabs.jsx ControlTab handler (legacy workflow). All stages open within the same Stitch shell.
Generic: each workflow stage (search, screening, extraction, analysis, grade, report, control, etc.) opens as ?tab=<id> within the unified shell. Derived from TABS array in projectHelpers.js.

behaviors.name: Dashboard loads with overview tab selected by default
behaviors.description: Navigating to /app (no ?view param) defaults to overview tab showing KPI cards and active project list.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to /app, verify page title contains 'Workspace Overview' and KPI cards are visible. Verify 4 metric cards (Active projects, Owned by you, Studies imported, Screening records) are rendered.

behaviors.name: Dashboard view switching via menu
behaviors.description: Clicking menu items (My Work, Invitations, Archived, Resources) navigates to the corresponding view via ?view= param.
behaviors.howToTest: From /app, click 'Invitations' menu item. Verify URL is /app?view=invitations. Verify page title changes. Repeat for mywork, archived, resources.

behaviors.name: Project list filters work independently
behaviors.description: Clicking filter buttons (All/Active/In progress/Completed/Owned by me) filters the project list without navigation. Each filter shows a count next to the label.
behaviors.howToTest: From /app overview, verify All filter is active by default. Click 'Active' filter. Verify only projects with active status show. Verify count labels update. Click search to combine with filters.

behaviors.name: Project search filters in real-time
behaviors.description: Typing in the search input (placeholder='Search projects…') filters projects by name substring match (case-insensitive) in real-time without page navigation.
behaviors.howToTest: From /app, type 'Statins' in search. Verify project list updates to show only projects containing 'statins' (case-insensitive). Clear search. Verify all projects return.

behaviors.name: Create project modal opens from button
behaviors.description: Clicking 'New project' button (in header or empty state) opens StitchModal with title 'New project', focus trap active, Escape key closes.
behaviors.howToTest: From /app overview, click 'New project' button. Verify modal opens. Verify modal has title 'New project' (h2 aria-labelledby). Verify focus moves to title input. Press Escape. Verify modal closes.

behaviors.name: Create project requires non-empty title
behaviors.description: Submitting the create form with an empty or whitespace-only title shows error message 'Please enter a project title.' and does not submit.
behaviors.howToTest: Open create modal. Leave title empty. Click 'Create project'. Verify error text appears. Type spaces. Click 'Create project'. Verify error text persists. Type valid name. Verify error clears. Submit succeeds.

behaviors.name: Create project with description
behaviors.description: The create modal accepts an optional description field. Submitting with description creates a project with that metadata.
behaviors.howToTest: Open create modal. Enter title 'Test Project'. Enter description 'A test review'. Click 'Create project'. Verify POST /api/projects succeeds. Verify project appears in list with description metadata.

behaviors.name: Create project shows loading state
behaviors.description: While submitting, the 'Create project' button shows loading spinner (aria-busy=true) and both buttons are disabled.
behaviors.howToTest: Open create modal. Enter valid title. Click 'Create project'. During submission, verify button shows spinner. Verify both Cancel and Create buttons are disabled. After success, verify modal closes.

behaviors.name: Create project success shows toast
behaviors.description: After successful project creation, a success toast message appears ('Project created' with green tone).
behaviors.howToTest: Create a project. Verify toast appears with success tone. Verify toast auto-dismisses. Verify project list reloads with new project.

behaviors.name: Create project creates linked screening workspace
behaviors.description: When creating a project, the API call includes createLinkedSift=true, which auto-creates a linked META.SIFT screening workspace.
behaviors.howToTest: Create a new project via modal. Verify api.projects.create() was called with createLinkedSift: true. Verify project._linkedMetaSift is populated after creation. Verify screening section renders in project overview.

behaviors.name: Project card open button navigates to project
behaviors.description: Clicking the 'Open' button on a project card navigates to /app/project/:id.
behaviors.howToTest: From /app overview, click 'Open' button on any project card. Verify navigation to /app/project/:projectId. Verify StitchProjectOverview page loads.

behaviors.name: Project card title displays with ellipsis on overflow
behaviors.description: Project titles longer than card width truncate with ellipsis (overflow:hidden, text-overflow:ellipsis, white-space:nowrap). The full title appears in title attribute on hover.
behaviors.howToTest: Create a project with a very long name (>60 chars). Verify card displays title with ellipsis. Hover over title. Verify tooltip or title attribute shows full name.

behaviors.name: Project card shows status badge
behaviors.description: Each project card displays a status badge (active/in_progress/done/archived) with the appropriate tone (brand/info/success/neutral).
behaviors.howToTest: View projects in dashboard. Verify each card has a status badge matching its lifecycle status. Verify badge tone matches STATUS_META[statusOf(p)]. Archive a project. Verify badge changes to 'Archived' with neutral tone.

behaviors.name: Project card shows role and member counts
behaviors.description: Project cards display role badge (Owner/Editor/Viewer), study count, record count (for linked screening), member count (for linked screening), and relative update time.
behaviors.howToTest: View projects. Verify role badge shows correct role. Verify '5 studies' text displays. For projects with linked screening, verify 'X records' and 'Y members' display. Verify 'updated X min ago' displays.

behaviors.name: Project card rename button is restricted by permissions
behaviors.description: The rename button (pencil icon) only shows for users with canEdit permission. Owners can always edit. Others depend on their role.
behaviors.howToTest: As owner, verify rename button shows. As editor, verify rename button shows. As viewer, verify rename button does not show. Verify aria-label='Rename'.

behaviors.name: Project card archive button is owner-only
behaviors.description: The archive/restore button only shows for project owners.
behaviors.howToTest: View project as owner. Verify archive button shows with layers or refresh icon. As non-owner, verify button is hidden.

behaviors.name: Project card delete button is owner-only
behaviors.description: The delete button (trash icon, ghost variant, red color) only shows for project owners.
behaviors.howToTest: View project as owner. Verify delete button shows (trash icon, red text, ghost variant). As non-owner, verify button is hidden.

behaviors.name: Rename project modal opens and pre-fills name
behaviors.description: Clicking the rename button on a project card opens ActionModal with type='rename'. The modal title is 'Rename project'. The name input is pre-filled with the current project name.
behaviors.howToTest: Click rename button on any project card. Verify modal title is 'Rename project'. Verify input field has the current project name pre-selected. Focus is on the input.

behaviors.name: Rename project requires non-empty name
behaviors.description: Clearing the project title and submitting shows error 'Title cannot be empty.'
behaviors.howToTest: Open rename modal. Clear the input. Click 'Save'. Verify error message appears. Type a new name. Verify error clears. Submit succeeds.

behaviors.name: Rename project updates in real-time
behaviors.description: After renaming, the project list refreshes with the new name. No page reload needed.
behaviors.howToTest: Rename a project via modal. Verify toast shows 'Project renamed'. Verify project list reloads. Verify the project card shows the new name. Verify reload via api.projects.list().

behaviors.name: Archive project modal shows confirmation
behaviors.description: Clicking the archive button opens ActionModal with type='archive'. The modal displays a confirmation message explaining what will happen.
behaviors.howToTest: Click archive button on a project. Verify modal title is 'Archive project?'. Verify text explains the project will be hidden and made read-only. Verify it can be restored later.

behaviors.name: Archive project hides from active list
behaviors.description: After archiving, the project disappears from the active project list on the overview tab and appears in the Archived Projects view.
behaviors.howToTest: Note a project in the overview. Click archive. Verify project disappears from overview list. Navigate to Archived Projects view. Verify project appears there.

behaviors.name: Restore project modal shows confirmation
behaviors.description: Clicking the restore button (for archived projects) opens ActionModal with type='unarchive'. The modal displays a confirmation message.
behaviors.howToTest: Go to Archived Projects view. Click restore button on an archived project. Verify modal title is 'Restore project?'. Verify text explains the project will return to active list.

behaviors.name: Restore project returns to active list
behaviors.description: After restoring an archived project, it re-appears in the active project list and disappears from Archived Projects.
behaviors.howToTest: In Archived Projects, click restore. Verify modal closes. Verify project disappears from Archived list. Go to overview. Verify project re-appears in active list.

behaviors.name: Delete project modal requires name confirmation
behaviors.description: Clicking the delete button opens DeleteProjectModal. The modal displays a danger warning, shows the project name in plain text, and requires the user to type the exact name to enable the Delete button.
behaviors.howToTest: Click delete button on a project. Verify modal title is 'Delete project'. Verify a red warning box explains deletion. Verify project name is shown. Verify a separate input requires typing the exact name. Verify Delete button is disabled until text matches exactly.

behaviors.name: Delete project name matching is case-sensitive
behaviors.description: The delete confirmation matching is case-sensitive (e.g., 'Test' does not match 'test'). Leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed from the input before comparison.
behaviors.howToTest: Open delete modal for project named 'TestProj'. Type 'testproj'. Verify Delete button stays disabled. Type 'TestProj'. Verify Delete button enables. Type ' TestProj ' (with spaces). Verify Delete button enables (spaces are trimmed).

behaviors.name: Delete project shows linked workspace warning
behaviors.description: If a project has a linked screening workspace (_linkedMetaSift is truthy), the delete warning explains that both the project and the linked workspace will be deleted.
behaviors.howToTest: Delete a project with a linked screening workspace. Verify warning text mentions 'imported records, decisions and members' will be deleted. Delete a project without linked workspace. Verify warning does not mention linked workspace.

behaviors.name: Delete project requires exact name match to activate button
behaviors.description: The Delete button is only enabled when confirmText (trimmed) exactly matches the project name. Partial matches do not enable the button.
behaviors.howToTest: Open delete modal for 'Full Review Title'. Type 'Full Review'. Verify Delete button is disabled. Type 'Full Review Title'. Verify Delete button becomes enabled. Delete 'Full' only. Verify button disables again.

behaviors.name: Delete project clears input when modal reopens
behaviors.description: When the delete modal closes and reopens (e.g., user cancels then tries again), the confirmation input is cleared.
behaviors.howToTest: Open delete modal. Type the project name. Click Cancel. Click delete again. Verify confirmation input is empty and Delete button is disabled.

behaviors.name: Delete project shows loading state
behaviors.description: While deleting, the Delete button shows loading spinner and both buttons are disabled. Cancel button is also disabled during deletion.
behaviors.howToTest: Open delete modal. Type name. Click Delete. During submission, verify button shows spinner and both Cancel and Delete are disabled. After success, verify modal closes.

behaviors.name: Delete project success message and refresh
behaviors.description: After successful deletion, a success toast shows ('Project deleted') and the project list refreshes (the deleted project disappears).
behaviors.howToTest: Delete a project. Verify success toast appears. Verify project list reloads. Verify deleted project is no longer in the list.

behaviors.name: Project overview page loads project data

behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to a project. Verify StitchProjectOverview renders. Verify project title is in the header. Verify loading state briefly shows then resolves. Verify all sections load (phases, metrics, team).

behaviors.name: Empty state displays when no projects exist

behaviors.howToTest: For a new user with no projects, navigate to /app. Verify empty state displays instead of project list. Verify 'New project' button is present and clickable. Click it. Verify create modal opens.

behaviors.name: KPI cards update when projects change
behaviors.description: The KPI metric cards (Active projects, Owned by you, Studies imported, Screening records) update in real-time when the project list changes.
behaviors.howToTest: View KPI cards showing initial counts. Create a new project. Verify card counts update. Archive a project. Verify 'Active projects' count decreases.

behaviors.name: Progress ring updates when phases complete

behaviors.howToTest: View progress ring. Mark phases as complete in projects. Verify ring percentage increases. Verify sublabel shows 'Complete'.

behaviors.name: Recently updated list shows most recent projects first
behaviors.description: The 'Recently updated' card shows up to 6 projects sorted by updatedAt descending. Clicking one opens the project.
behaviors.howToTest: View recently updated list. Verify projects are ordered newest first. Update a project. Verify it moves to top of list. Click a project. Verify navigation to /app/project/:id.

seeding.what: Admin user account with Stitch UI access
seeding.how: global-setup.ts runs one-time, creates or logs in an admin user, stores auth session in .playwright/auth/admin.json, sets localStorage metalab_ui_design=stitch, and persists via /api/profile PUT. All tests import from stitch-test.ts which auto-applies this storageState + primeStitch().

seeding.what: Seed project (pre-created for smoke tests, optional for feature tests)
seeding.how: global-setup.ts calls api.projects.create() if SEED_PROJECT_ID env var not set, stores result in .playwright/auth/seed.json { seedProjectId, adminEmail, baseURL, apiURL }. Individual tests can read seed via the `seed` fixture or create tmpProject fixture for isolated CRUD tests.

seeding.what: Temporary test project (auto-created and deleted per test)
seeding.how: tmpProject fixture in stitch-test.ts calls createProject(request, name), yields { id, name }, then deleteProject(request, id) in cleanup. Use this for all create/edit/delete flows to avoid touching the seed project.
createProject() API call includes createLinkedSift: true by default (see StitchDashboard.jsx line 74). Seeded projects automatically have _linkedMetaSift populated.

Use api.projects.update() to set custom status/phases on a project, or seed multiple projects with statusOf() and STATUS_META combinations (active, in_progress, done, archived). See projectLanding.helpers.js for helpers.

seeding.what: Multiple projects with long names (for overflow testing)
seeding.how: When seeding, create projects with names >60 chars (e.g., 'A very long project name that should truncate with ellipsis when displayed in the card title'). Test overflow and tooltip display.

seeding.what: Admin user as owner vs. non-owner context
seeding.how: E2E suite runs as admin (always owner). To test non-owner views, either mock API responses or invite a second test user with a specific role via api.projects.addMember(). Alternative: mock canEdit/isOwner in test.

gaps: Stitch primitive components (StitchModal, StitchButton, StitchCard, etc.) in src/frontend/stitch/primitives/ have no data-testid attributes. Many rely on getByRole() + accessible name, but complex nested elements may need manual testids (e.g., modal footer buttons, error messages in forms). Suggested: add data-testid to modal title, modal footer buttons, form error spans.

Project card action buttons (rename, archive, delete) use only aria-label and icons. No data-testids. Selectors work but are fragile to icon name changes. Suggested: add data-testid to all three action buttons on ProjectRow component.

Dashboard menu items are buttons with icon + text but no data-testid. Relying on getByRole('button', { name: /.../ }) works but is brittle if labels change. Suggested: add data-testid='menu-item-<view>' (e.g., menu-item-overview, menu-item-mywork).
KPI metric cards are divs with text labels but no structured testids. Test must find by text content. Suggested: add data-testid='metric-card-<label>' (e.g., metric-card-active-projects).

Project card itself is a .stitch-card with no unique identifier. Tests must find by project name text or grid position. For reliable matching of a specific project in a list, rely on project name text or add data-testid='project-card-<projectId>' at render time.

Filter buttons have no data-testid. Tests rely on getByRole('button', { name: /All|Active|In progress|.../ }). Suggested: add data-testid='filter-<key>' (e.g., filter-all, filter-active, filter-inprogress).

Project header title in ProjectHeaderBar (legacy workspace) is a button with inline style and minWidth:0. No testid. Works via getByRole('button') but title truncation logic and overflow ellipsis are visual-only (no semantic indication in DOM).

Autosave/persistence of localStorage state (filter, search) is tested implicitly (page refresh and state persists), but there's no explicit test of the localStorage.setItem calls or timing. Consider adding a spy on localStorage.
Modal focus trap uses internal ref and useEffect. No observable testid for focus state. Test must verify aria-modal, focus location, and Escape key behavior indirectly.
Empty states use generic text. No data-testid. Tests must rely on getByText() for 'No projects yet', 'No matching projects', etc.

Feature flags like aiScreening, rob_engine_v2 are server-side and not directly tested in Stitch dashboard/project lifecycle (they affect downstream workflow stages). Smoke test checks searchEngine + pecanSearch presence, but no end-to-end flag toggle test.

Delete confirmation input validation (case-sensitive, whitespace trim) is implemented in JS but not visually indicated. No aria-current or aria-disabled feedback on the input itself. Test must verify button disabled state indirectly.

Real-time updates via useRealtime() (project.updated, members.changed events) are tested by mocking server events, but there's no stable way to assert that a specific update triggered a data refresh without checking API call logs or using a network spy.
Members tab (StitchProjectOverview) renders member list from screeningApi.listMembers(). No pagination or virtualization yet. If many members exist, tests must account for list height/scroll.

Linked screening workspace creation (createLinkedSift: true) is assumed to succeed synchronously. If the server makes this async (deferred creation), tests may need to poll or wait for a server event.
Project card progress bar (percentage width animation) has inline style transitions. No testid. Visual regression tests are needed to verify the bar displays the correct percentage.

map.summary: The AdminConsole.jsx (~8200 lines) is a comprehensive operations control panel accessible at /ops (admin-only) or /sift-beta (mod-only with limited tabs). It uses a fixed sidebar navigation with 16 major tabs. State is managed with useState for the active tab (default: 'overview' for admin, 'users' for mod). Server validates allowed sections via GET /api/admin/console. Each tab is a self-contained section component (OverviewSection, UsersSection, etc.) that lazily loads data via adminApi client. Persistence follows a consistent pattern: load via GET, modify local state, save via PUT/PATCH, show status badge (saving/saved/error with 3s timeout). The Appearance tab is the only place currently managing design/theme at scale; designSettings SiteSetting (allowAllUsers, defaultMode for Stitch UI rollout governance) is NOT YET implemented in Ops UI but the backend infrastructure (designMode.js resolveDesignMode function) expects it to be configurable via SiteSetting.

routes.notes: Main admin ops console. Entry point at line 7998. Server validates role + allowed sections via /api/admin/console (line 8017)
Alias for /ops in Stitch UI context. Mods see limited console (users + messages tabs only)

uiElements.name: Sidebar nav - Risk of Bias tab
Overview - Active brand swatch + theme name button

behaviors.name: Tab navigation
behaviors.description: Clicking a sidebar nav button updates state.active and renders the corresponding section. Active tab has background color alpha(C.acc, '14') and left border C.acc. Mod users only see users + messages tabs (enforced server-side via /api/admin/console).
behaviors.howToTest: Click 'Settings' tab → verify SettingsSection renders. Click 'Flags' → verify FlagsSection renders. When logged in as mod, verify Flags/Security/Health tabs are not visible in sidebar.

behaviors.name: Appearance tab - Live brand color preview
behaviors.description: Entering a valid hex color immediately previews the theme across the whole console (via useTheme().previewBrand()). A 'Live preview active' banner shows with 'Revert preview' button. On tab switch/unmount, preview reverts to saved brand via clearBrandPreview().
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Appearance tab. Enter '#ff0000' in hex input → verify console accent color changes to red immediately. Click 'Revert preview' → verify color reverts. Navigate away from Appearance tab → verify color reverts on unmount.

behaviors.name: Appearance tab - Theme save
behaviors.description: Save button (line 4756) calls adminApi.theme.save({ brandColor, preset, palette }) → PATCH /api/admin/settings/theme. Button shows 'saving' state (with spinner), then 'saved' (3s timeout), then reverts to 'idle'. updatedAt is displayed as 'Last changed [date]'.
behaviors.howToTest: Enter a valid hex color. Click 'Save theme'. Verify POST spinner shows, then 'Saved' badge for 3s. Verify 'Last changed' date updates. Refresh page → verify brand color persists.

behaviors.name: Appearance tab - Reset to default
behaviors.description: Reset button calls adminApi.theme.save({ reset: true }) → same endpoint, server returns default theme record. UI resets hex input, preset, and updatedAt.
behaviors.howToTest: Save a custom brand color. Click 'Reset to default'. Verify status shows 'Saved', hex input reverts to #4f46e5, preset shows 'default'. Refresh → verify default theme persists.

behaviors.name: Feature Flags - Toggle and save
behaviors.description: Each flag is a Toggle component (line 4818). Toggling locally updates state.flags[key]. Save button calls adminApi.featureFlags.save(flags) → PUT /api/admin/feature-flags. Status badge shows saving/saved/error.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Flags tab. Toggle 'Risk of Bias (RoB 2)' ON. Click 'Save Changes'. Verify status shows 'Saved' for 3s. Refresh page → verify flag state persists.

behaviors.name: Feature Flags - Dependency warning
behaviors.description: If a flag has a 'requires' dep (e.g. pecanSearch requires searchEngine), and the dep is OFF but the flag is ON, a yellow warning banner shows: '⚠ Inactive: enable "X" first'.
behaviors.howToTest: Toggle 'Pecan Search Engine' ON without enabling 'Search Builder Engine' first. Verify yellow warning appears: '⚠ Inactive: enable "Search Builder Engine" first'.

behaviors.name: Settings tab - Load and save app settings
behaviors.description: SettingsSection loads via adminApi.settings.get() (line 4366) → GET /api/admin/settings. Form fields (appName, registrationOpen, etc.) are merged server response into state.form. Save calls adminApi.settings.save(body) → PUT /api/admin/settings. On success, re-merges server response.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Settings tab. Change 'App Name' to 'TestApp'. Click 'Save'. Verify status shows 'Saved'. Refresh → verify 'TestApp' persists.

behaviors.name: Settings tab - Load failed state
behaviors.description: If initial GET /api/admin/settings fails, loadFailed=true, save button is disabled, and a red NoticeBox warns 'Settings could not be loaded — saving is disabled'.
behaviors.howToTest: Simulate network error on GET /api/admin/settings (e.g. via DevTools network throttle). Navigate to Settings → verify red error banner and disabled save button.

behaviors.name: Overview tab - KPI cards with sparklines
behaviors.description: Overview renders 6 KPI cards (Total Users, Total Projects, Online/Offline Users, Unread Messages, Failed Logins). Each card shows a value + sub-stat + optional sparkline from 14-day trend data (metricsTimeseries). Cards clickable to navigate to related tabs (e.g. unread messages → Messages tab).
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Overview. Verify KPI cards render with values. Click 'Unread Messages' card → verify navigates to Messages tab. Hover over sparkline area → sparkline data should align with 14-day window.

behaviors.name: Overview tab - Activity chart + system health grid
behaviors.description: Two-column grid: left = AreaChart with 14-day logins/new users/new projects/screening decisions (from metricsTimeseries). Right = System Health with 2x3 grid of backend/db/env/version/uptime/last-event tiles. Health live indicator pulses if EventSource /api/events is connected.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Overview. Verify Activity chart loads with 4 series. Verify System Health shows OK badges. Listen for live events → 'live' indicator should pulse, 'Last Event' should show 'listening…' then 'just now' as events arrive.

behaviors.name: Overview tab - Screening pipeline funnel + completion donut
behaviors.description: Screening Pipeline shows a FunnelBar with stages (Records → Screened → 2nd Review → To Extraction). Completion shows a DonutGauge (Done %, In Progress %, Not Started %) + 3 sub-tiles (Done Today/Week/Month counts).
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Overview. Verify screening funnel loads. Click on a stage → tooltip should show details. Verify donut chart shows done %, sub-tiles show daily/weekly/monthly counts.

behaviors.name: Message read badge on sidebar
behaviors.description: Messages nav button (line 8131) displays a yellow badge with unread count when unread > 0. Badge sourced from adminApi.messages.unreadCount() (line 8039). Clears when message is opened (mark-read).
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Messages tab. Verify unread badge appears on sidebar 'Messages' button. Open a message → verify badge count decrements (or disappears if was last unread).

behaviors.name: Users tab - List, detail panel, edit panel
behaviors.description: UsersSection renders a DataTable of users (line 1879 UserDetailPanel). Clicking a row opens an edit panel on the right with fields from editableFieldsForRole schema. Save via adminApi.users.update(id, body) → PATCH /api/admin/users/:id.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Users tab. Verify user table loads. Click a user row → detail panel opens on right. Edit a field (e.g. role). Click Save → verify status shows 'Saved'. Refresh → verify change persists.

behaviors.name: Projects tab - Project detail & archive
behaviors.description: ProjectsSection (line 3355 ProjectDetailPanel) renders a searchable/sortable table of projects. Clicking a project opens detail panel with options to archive/restore. Admin can view linked Screening project if exists.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Projects tab. Click a project → detail panel opens. Click 'Archive' → verify status updates to archived. Refresh → verify archived state persists.

behaviors.name: Screening tab - Pipeline health, engine settings, AI policy
behaviors.description: SiftAdminSection (line 5895 SiftProjectDetailPanel) shows workspace health, per-project progress detail, and AI Screening policy. AI policy lives in AiScreeningSection (line 4855) with toggles for enabled/killswitch, thresholds, embedding provider. Save via adminApi.aiScreening.saveSettings().
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Screening tab. Verify workspace health loads. Toggle 'AI screening enabled' ON. Click Save → verify persists. Change embedding provider to 'hashing' → verify persists.

behaviors.name: Risk of Bias tab - RoB settings & metrics
behaviors.description: RobAdminSection shows engine-level settings (requireHumanFinalDecision, allowReviewersToRun, etc.) and metrics (projects using RoB, assessment counts, conflict summaries). Save via adminApi.rob.saveSettings().
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Risk of Bias tab. Verify settings load. Change a setting (e.g. requireHumanFinalDecision toggle). Click Save → verify persists and audit log shows UPDATE_ROB_SETTINGS.

behaviors.name: Beta Waitlist tab - Applicant list, status updates, notes
behaviors.description: WaitlistSection (line 220 in adminApiClient) lists applicants with search/filter/pagination. Clicking an applicant opens detail modal. Status can be set via adminApi.betaWaitlist.setStatus(id, status, note) → PATCH. Export CSV via adminApi.betaWaitlist.exportUrl().
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Beta Waitlist tab. Verify applicants load. Click an applicant → modal opens. Change status to 'approved'. Click Save → verify persists. Click 'Export CSV' → verify download starts.

behaviors.name: Content tab - Landing page content editor
behaviors.description: ContentSection (line 4500+) edits landingContent SiteSetting via adminApi.landingContent.get/save() → GET/PUT /api/admin/landing-content. Rich text editor for hero, CTA, etc.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Content tab. Edit 'Hero Title' field. Click Save → verify status shows 'Saved'. Refresh → verify change persists. Visit landing page (/) → verify new title displays.

behaviors.name: Engine Versions tab - Version list + history modal
behaviors.description: EngineVersionsSection lists all engines with versions (major.minor), status (active/deprecated/beta), change type (major/minor), last change summary, updated timestamp. Clicking 'History' opens modal showing full changelog. Data from adminApi.engineVersions.list() and .history(id).
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Engine Versions tab. Verify engines load. Click 'History' button on an engine → modal opens showing version changelog.

behaviors.name: Messages tab - Inbox, reply, compose
behaviors.description: MessagesSection shows unread contact submissions. Clicking a message opens detail panel with full message + replies. Can reply (PATCH) or compose new email (POST /api/admin/emails). Both trigger mark-read to clear unread badge.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Messages tab. Verify unread messages appear. Click a message → detail panel opens. Reply with text. Click Send → verify reply sent, unread badge decrements.

behaviors.name: Security tab - Audit log, security events, summary
behaviors.description: SecuritySection shows auditLog (all admin actions), securityEvents (failed logins, rate limits, access denied), and securitySummary (summary stats). Filter by date range and action type. No save — read-only view.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Security tab. Verify audit log loads with recent actions. Filter by action = 'UPDATE_FEATURE_FLAGS' → verify results filtered. Verify security events show failed logins.

behaviors.name: Health tab - System health snapshot + diagnostics
behaviors.description: HealthSection (line 7000+) shows backend/db/environment/version/uptime/events from health check. Also shows error rates, database query performance, and memory usage if available.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to Health tab. Verify health metrics load. Verify DB status shows 'OK'. Uptime shows hours + minutes. No save functionality.

behaviors.name: Server validation of role + sections
behaviors.description: On mount, AdminConsole calls adminApi.console() → GET /api/admin/console. Server returns { role, sections, emailConfigured }. Client updates state.role and state.allowed (Set of section ids). If fetch fails, falls back to role-derived defaults (Admin → all sections, Mod → [users, messages]). Mods never see admin-only tabs even as dead links.
behaviors.howToTest: Log in as admin. Verify all 16 tabs visible in sidebar. Log out, log in as mod. Verify only 'Users' and 'Messages' tabs visible. Attempt direct URL navigation to /ops?section=flags → mod should see AccessDenied panel.

behaviors.name: Persistence of theme after refresh
behaviors.description: Brand color is saved via adminApi.theme.save() → PATCH /api/admin/settings/theme (line 4658). Server persists to SiteSetting. On app load, ThemeContext.useTheme() queries the brand from context (which was hydrated from server). Refresh preserves brand across browser sessions.
behaviors.howToTest: Save brand color #ff5733 in Appearance tab. Refresh entire page. Verify brand color still #ff5733. Check DevTools Network → /api/admin/settings/theme should show saved record.

behaviors.name: Persistence of feature flags after refresh
behaviors.description: Feature flags are saved via adminApi.featureFlags.save(flags) → PUT /api/admin/feature-flags (line 4791). Server persists to SiteSetting. On app load, flags are loaded via GET /api/admin/feature-flags. Refresh preserves all flag state.
behaviors.howToTest: Toggle 'Network Meta-Analysis' ON in Flags tab. Refresh page. Verify flag is still ON. Check server SiteSetting record → featureFlags.networkMetaAnalysis should be true.

seeding.how: Register as normal user. Server promotes first user to admin (or use db script UPDATE User SET role='admin' WHERE email='test@example.com'). Log in → navigate to /ops → verify all 16 tabs visible in sidebar.
