seeding.how: Create admin user first. Then admin invites normal user. Admin runs UPDATE User SET role='mod' WHERE id='...' in db. Mod logs in → navigate to /ops → verify only 'Users' and 'Messages' tabs visible.

seeding.what: Custom brand color in Appearance tab
seeding.how: Log in as admin. Navigate to Appearance tab. Enter hex color #4f46e5 in custom color input. Click 'Save theme'. Verify status shows 'Saved'. Brand color is now persisted to SiteSetting.
Log in as admin. Navigate to Flags tab. Toggle 'AI Screening Engine' ON. Click 'Save Changes'. Flag is now persisted to SiteSetting featureFlags.aiScreening=true.
Log in as admin. Navigate to Settings tab. Change 'App Name' to custom value. Click 'Save'. AppSettings.appName is persisted to SiteSetting.

User submits contact form (on /beta-waitlist or landing). Server creates ContactMessage with read=false. Admin logs in → Ops → Messages → unread badge appears on sidebar. Click message → detail opens, click 'Mark as read' → badge clears.
betaWaitlist feature flag ON. Visitor goes to /beta-waitlist (instead of /). Fill form, submit. Server creates BetaWaitlistApplicant. Admin logs in → Ops → Beta Waitlist → applicants list loads.
User creates a Workspace + linked ScreenProject. Adds records. Reviewers screen some records. Admin logs in → Ops → Overview → Screening Pipeline funnel + Completion donut should load with data.

gaps: designSettings UI controls (allowAllUsers toggle, defaultMode dropdown) are NOT YET implemented in AdminConsole. The backend infrastructure exists (designMode.js resolveDesignMode function expects these via SiteSetting), but Ops console has no form fields to edit them. Recommend adding to Appearance tab after brand color section (see implementation notes below).
No test IDs on most interactive elements — selectors rely on visible text/role attributes. Consider adding data-testid to all major buttons/inputs for selector stability.
Pagination is implemented client-side for Users/Projects tables but not documented — max 50 rows per page hardcoded. No server-side pagination indicator visible.

Real-time updates (e.g. new users registered) require manual 'Refresh' click on Overview — no auto-poll or WebSocket for live counts. Feed refresh is debounced on EventSource /api/events but data is stale by 4s.
No bulk actions (e.g. bulk-suspend users, bulk-change feature flags). Every change is one-by-one.
Security audit trail (AdminAuditLog) is queryable but Ops UI does not wire auditFilter form to all query params — only shows hardcoded 'UPDATE_' actions. Granular filtering not exposed.
Export/import of settings (backup/restore Ops config) not available — no way to clone ops state between deployments.
Onboarding & RoB tabs exist in NAV_SECTIONS but implementation not fully visible in excerpt — validate tab loads and persists correctly.

map.summary: The Screening engine is PecanRev's collaborative title/abstract and full-text review workbench (META·SIFT). It consists of a tabbed project shell (/sift-beta/projects/:pid) hosting seven sub-engines (Overview, Title & Abstract, Final Review, Duplicates, Conflicts, Settings, Export). The core screening surface is a 3-column layout: left (record list with search/filter), middle (selected record detail with PICO-highlighted abstract, PDF, AI assistance, and decision buttons), right (PICO question, keyword filters with per-term article counts, study-type filter, labels/reasons vocabulary). AI scoring (aiScreening flag) provides TF-IDF + active-learning relevance ranking, gated at 50 screened decisions (admin-overridable), with inline explanations + "why this score" breakdown. Keyboard shortcuts (configurable per-user, persisted to profile) control navigation and decisions. Duplicate detection runs synchronously or on-demand before the Title & Abstract stage. Second-review (Final Review tab) surfaces records that met quorum (2 include votes) for full-text screening. Conflicts tab shows disputed records (reviewer disagreement). Two-reviewer quorum + conflict resolution enable multi-rater workflow. Export CSV/JSON/RIS in batches (async for large projects). All state syncs live via realtime events (decision.saved, ai.updated, project.updated) without full-page reloads.

featureFlags.howToEnable: Admin feature flag. Enables AI Screening Intelligence engine. Detected via GET /api/screening/:pid/ai/status (404 = flag OFF).
Server persistence for workflow state instead of localStorage. Affects PICO/protocol state storage.

behaviors.name: Open screening from project
behaviors.description: Navigate to /sift-beta or embedded Screening tab. ScreeningTab renders 3-column layout.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to /sift-beta/projects/:pid?tab=screening. Assert left/middle/right columns render. Verify left column has sticky search/filter header.

behaviors.name: Import multiple file formats
behaviors.description: Support RIS, PubMed/NBIB, BibTeX, CSV, Web of Science, EndNote, plain text. Auto-detect or force explicit format. Large imports queue async job with progress polling.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to ?tab=import. Test each format: select format, upload file, preview 5 records, click Import. For large file >5000 records: assert progress bar (Validating → Parsing → Deduplicating). Assert results show counts.

behaviors.name: Duplicate detection and resolution
behaviors.description: Run detection (DOI exact, PMID, fuzzy title). Unresolved groups shown first. Select primary record. Merge or Keep All.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to ?tab=duplicates. Click Detect Duplicates (leader-only). Assert groups created. Select primary radio for group. Click Merge. Assert group resolves.

behaviors.name: Search and filter records
behaviors.description: Type to search (debounced 300ms) title/author/DOI. Dropdown filter: all, unopened_me, opened_me, undecided, included/excluded/maybe, quorum, disputed. Both narrow left column list.
behaviors.howToTest: Type search term → assert results filter. Change dropdown filter → assert list narrows. Combine search + filter → both apply.

behaviors.name: Select record and mark opened
behaviors.description: Click record row → middle column shows detail. First click marks myOpened=true.
behaviors.howToTest: Click unopened record → assert middle column renders + row 'NEW' badge → dot.

behaviors.name: Make include/exclude/maybe decision
behaviors.description: Click Include/Exclude/Maybe → auto-save (toggle=undo). Exclude → reason field appears. Save persists to DB + row badge updates.
behaviors.howToTest: Click Include → button green, message shows. Click again → undo. Click Exclude → reason field visible. Type reason → click Save.

behaviors.name: Keyboard shortcuts
behaviors.description: User-configurable per profile. Default: I=include, E=exclude, M=maybe, U=undo. Blocked while typing in inputs.
behaviors.howToTest: Press I → Include toggles. Type in Notes textarea → press I → shortcut blocked. Blur textarea → press I → shortcut triggered.

behaviors.name: Two-reviewer quorum
behaviors.description: 2+ reviewers mark include → quorumMet=true, record eligible for Final Review.
behaviors.howToTest: Seed 2 members. A & B both include same record. Reload → record shows 'Quorum' badge.

behaviors.name: Conflict resolution
behaviors.description: Reviewers disagree → disputed=true. Leader resolves in Conflicts tab by selecting final decision.
behaviors.howToTest: Seed A includes, B excludes. Navigate to ?tab=conflicts. Find record. Leader selects include. Assert record leaves conflicts tab.

behaviors.name: Add/manage labels
behaviors.description: Leader: add label. Members: toggle labels per record. Persisted in decision.labels.
behaviors.howToTest: Leader: add label 'high-quality'. Member: open record, toggle label, click Save. Reload → label persisted.

behaviors.name: Add/manage exclusion reasons
behaviors.description: Leader: define reasons. Members: select from dropdown or type free text. Toggle 'Save as predefined' → creates vocab entry.
behaviors.howToTest: Leader: add reason 'Not in English'. Member: exclude record, select reason from dropdown.

behaviors.name: Quality rating (stars)
behaviors.description: Click star 1-5 to set rating. Click same star to clear (=0). Persisted in decision.rating.
behaviors.howToTest: Click 3rd star → rating=3. Click 3rd again → rating=0. Set rating=5, save, reload → persisted.

behaviors.name: PICO highlighting in abstract
behaviors.description: Toggle 'Inclusion (green)' and 'Exclusion (red)' independent. When ON, keywords highlighted. Both can be OFF.
behaviors.howToTest: Open record with keywords in abstract. Assert green/red highlights. Toggle inclusion OFF → green gone. Toggle exclusion OFF → red gone.

behaviors.name: Keyword filter with article counts
behaviors.description: Right column shows each keyword + count matching that keyword. Check keyword → filters record list (OR logic).
behaviors.howToTest: Right column shows 'diabetes 23' checkbox. Check it → list filters to records with 'diabetes'. Check 'hypertension' → records with either keyword show (OR).

behaviors.name: Progress stats
behaviors.description: Overview tab or stepper shows workflow progress: counts by stage (imported, screened, quorum).
behaviors.howToTest: Seed 10 records imported. Open Overview → 'Imported: 10'. Screen 2 records → 'Screened: 2' updates (realtime).

behaviors.name: AI relevance score (aiScreening ON)
behaviors.description: AiScoreCard shows ranking score, prediction, confidence/uncertainty bars, calibrated probability, 'Why this score?' expandable.
behaviors.howToTest: Enable aiScreening flag. Seed 50+ screened records. Run AI scoring. Open record → AiScoreCard renders score 0-100, prediction chip. Click 'Why?' → explanations load.

behaviors.name: AI score gating (50-screened threshold)
behaviors.description: Below 50 screened: scores hidden, card shows 'X/50 screened' progress. Admin-only override button.
behaviors.howToTest: Enable aiScreening. Seed <50 screened records. Open record → AiScoreCard shows placeholder 'X/50'. If admin: override button visible.

behaviors.name: AI active-learning queues
behaviors.description: AiQueueBar: mode (default/high_confidence/uncertain/flagged), band (all/high/medium/low/uncertain). Reorders list.
behaviors.howToTest: Enable aiScreening + run scoring. Click mode 'high_confidence' → list reorders by score DESC. Click band 'uncertain' → only uncertain records show.

behaviors.name: Export CSV/JSON/RIS
behaviors.description: ExportTab: filter (all/included/excluded/maybe). Small project: sync download. Large project: async job, poll until ready.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to ?tab=export. Select filter 'Included'. Click Export → dialog. Select CSV → download starts (or progress updates).

behaviors.name: Blind mode
behaviors.description: Project setting: blindMode=true. Hides authors in rows/detail. Reviewer identity badges anonymized. AI scores hidden until decision made.

Seed base. PATCH /api/screening/:pid (blindMode: true). Verify authors hidden on reload.

seeding.how: Seed 2+ members. Both include same record. Record.quorumMet=true, currentStage=full_text.
Seed 2+ members. A includes, B excludes, same record. Record.disputed=true.
Seed project with similar records (same DOI or high fuzzy title match). POST /api/screening/:pid/detect-duplicates.

map.area: RoB + Data Extraction + Meta-Analysis Engines (PecanRev Systematic Review Platform)
map.summary: Three parallel assessment engines in PecanRev: (1) RoB 2 (Risk of Bias) is a standalone keyboard-first assessment workspace accessible via /rob/:projectId and embedded in the project workspace "Risk of Bias" tab, gated by rob_engine_v2 flag; (2) Data Extraction is embedded in workspace ?tab=extraction, features 2x2 risk-ratio fields, effect-size calculator with zero-cell continuity correction, and inline validation; (3) Network Meta-Analysis is embedded in workspace ?tab=nma, gated by networkMetaAnalysis flag, runs deterministic server-side analysis with forest plots, P-score ranking, and inconsistency detection. All three use the legacy UI by default; admin-only Stitch design (html[data-ui-design="stitch"]) is the primary E2E test surface. RoB persists to /api/rob/*; extraction/NMA autosave to project blob; all require admin seeding for feature flags (default OFF).

routes.notes: RoB project picker — lists all projects where user is owner or has canAssessRiskOfBias permission. Gated client-side on rob_engine_v2 flag.

Standalone RoB assessment workspace for one project. Shows robvis traffic-light summary, study list with assessment cards, tool selector, manual study CRUD. Opens RobWorkspace inline on study selection.
Data Extraction tab embedded in project workspace. Studies list with inline study editor, effect-size calculator, validation, and export.
Risk of Bias tab embedded in project workspace. Dispatcher: when rob_engine_v2=true renders ProjectRobPanel; when false renders LegacyRoBTab (per-study table).
Network Meta-Analysis tab embedded in project workspace. Gated on networkMetaAnalysis flag. Shows disabled note when OFF.
Returns the RoB 2 instrument definition (domains, questions, responses). Used to render the assessment form. Gated on rob_engine_v2 flag + project owner/canAssessRiskOfBias.
Returns [assessments] + matrix for robvis summary plot. Gated on rob_engine_v2 + access.
Returns merged study universe: screening-derived (from project.studies) + RoB manual studies. Gated on access.
Creates a new RoB assessment for a study. Requires projectId, studyId. Gated on canEdit + rob_engine_v2.
Returns full assessment: answers, domain judgments (proposed + final + override reason), overall judgment, completeness flag, summary matrix.
Upsert questions answers (per-question responses). Triggers recompute of proposed domain judgments. Autosave from RobWorkspace.
Override a domain's proposed judgment with a human-entered final judgment + justification. Gated on creator/owner/leader (canMutateAssessment).
Mark assessment as complete. Gated on creator/owner/leader.
Reopen a finalized assessment for editing. Gated on creator/owner/leader.
Export assessment as PDF, Word, or JSON. Query param: ?format=pdf|docx|json.
Validate NMA dataset live (no run). Returns { ok, errors[], warnings[], treatments[] }. Gated on networkMetaAnalysis flag.
Run the full NMA analysis (deterministic, server-side). Returns forest plot, P-scores, league table, inconsistency (node-split + global), contribution matrix. Gated on networkMetaAnalysis flag.

featureFlags.howToEnable: Ops Console › Feature Flags › rob_engine_v2 (toggle ON). Gated in settings controller default at L63. When ON: /rob routes render, /api/rob/* available, RoBTab renders ProjectRobPanel instead of LegacyRoBTab. When OFF: /rob shows NotEnabled page, RoBTab renders legacy per-study table.

Ops Console › Feature Flags › networkMetaAnalysis (toggle ON). Gated in settings controller default at L102. When ON: NmaTab renders full interface, /api/nma/* endpoints available, workspace shows NMA tab. When OFF: workspace NMA tab shows disabled note (text: 'Network Meta-Analysis is not enabled').
Gates whether extraction/NMA use per-module server-backed state (REST endpoints) vs. whole-project blob autosave. Default OFF keeps using blob autosave (current behavior). Optional for E2E.
Gates legacy standalone meta-analysis features (not NMA). Leave ON.

RoB tool selector (RoB 2 vs NOS dropdown/tabs)
Extraction effect-size type selector (SMD, OR, RR, RD, etc.)

behaviors.name: RoB engine activation (flag OFF)
behaviors.description: When rob_engine_v2=false, /rob shows NotEnabled page with admin instructions, and workspace RoBTab renders LegacyRoBTab (legacy table). API returns 404 for all /api/rob/* requests.
behaviors.howToTest: Set rob_engine_v2=false in Ops › Feature Flags. Navigate to /rob. Assert: NotEnabled page renders, text mentions 'rob_engine_v2', lock icon shown. Inspect /api/rob/instruments/rob2 → expect 404.

behaviors.name: RoB engine activation (flag ON)
behaviors.description: When rob_engine_v2=true, /rob shows project picker, /rob/:projectId opens ProjectRobPanel, workspace RoBTab renders new RoB engine.
behaviors.howToTest: Set rob_engine_v2=true. Navigate to /rob. Assert: project picker renders (list of projects). Click a project → ProjectRobPanel loads, shows traffic-light summary, study list, tool selector.

behaviors.name: RoB assessment creation
behaviors.description: User selects a study, clicks 'Start Assessment' or 'Create Assessment', RobWorkspace opens inline with domain form. POST /api/rob/assessments creates the record.
behaviors.howToTest: From ProjectRobPanel, click a study card. Assert: RobWorkspace opens, form loads with 5 domain sections (bias in randomization, bias in deviations, etc.), all questions visible, no answers selected yet.

behaviors.name: RoB question response save + autosave
behaviors.description: User clicks a response option (Yes/No/PNC) for a question. PUT /api/rob/assessments/:id/answers saves the response. Recomputes proposed domain judgment server-side. Optional: autosave on blur.
behaviors.howToTest: In RobWorkspace, select a question response (e.g., 'Yes' for RoB2 Q1a). Assert: PUT call fires to /api/rob/assessments/:id/answers with {answers: [{questionId, domainId, response}]}. No page reload. Domain judgment may auto-update if proposal changed.

behaviors.name: RoB domain judgment override
behaviors.description: User overrides the engine's proposed judgment (e.g., proposed 'Low' → override to 'Some concerns'). POST /api/rob/assessments/:id/override with {domainId, finalJudgment, justification}. Server stores override + justification.
behaviors.howToTest: In RobWorkspace domain section, click domain judgment button. Assert: text 'Proposed: Low' + override control appears. Enter new judgment (e.g., 'Some concerns') + justification text. POST /api/rob/assessments/:id/override fires. Assert: override flag set, final judgment persists on reload.

behaviors.name: RoB assessment finalization
behaviors.description: User clicks 'Finalise Assessment' button. POST /api/rob/assessments/:id/finalise marks the assessment complete. User can no longer edit unless they reopen.
behaviors.howToTest: Complete a few domain judgments. Scroll to bottom. Click 'Finalise'. Assert: POST to finalise endpoint fires. Assert: page navigates back to ProjectRobPanel, assessment card shows 'Complete' badge or lock icon. Reopening workspace shows 'Reopen' button instead of edit.

behaviors.name: RoB assessment export
behaviors.description: User clicks 'Export' dropdown, selects format (PDF, DOCX, JSON). GET /api/rob/assessments/:id/export?format=pdf downloads file.
behaviors.howToTest: In RobWorkspace or ProjectRobPanel assessment card, click 'Export'. Select 'PDF'. Assert: file download starts, Content-Disposition header = attachment. Verify PDF contains assessment data.

behaviors.name: RoB split-view PDF + assessment side-by-side
behaviors.description: When rob_engine_v2=true and PDF available, assessment form and PDF should render side-by-side on wide (>=900px) screens. Narrower screens stack vertically.
behaviors.howToTest: On desktop (1920x1080), open RobWorkspace. Assert: layout is flex row. PDF panel on left, form on right. Resize to 800px width. Assert: layout stacks vertically (flex column).

behaviors.name: RoB autosave + persistence
behaviors.description: User fills in assessment data (answers, overrides). Changes autosave to server. On page reload, assessment loads with all previous changes intact.
behaviors.howToTest: In RobWorkspace, change multiple question responses. Close workspace (back to ProjectRobPanel). Wait 2s (autosave debounce). Reload page. Reopen assessment. Assert: all answers and overrides from before reload are present.

behaviors.name: RoB read-only member access
behaviors.description: A workspace member granted canAssessRiskOfBias but WITHOUT canEdit sees all RoB UI in read-only mode (no editable controls). POST/PUT/DELETE return 403.
behaviors.howToTest: Create a read-only RoB member (in workspace settings). Log in as that member. Navigate to RoBTab. Assert: ProjectRobPanel renders, but buttons (Create Assessment, override, finalise) are disabled or hidden. 'View only' badge shown. POST to override → expect 403.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction page load
behaviors.description: User navigates to workspace ExtractionTab. Page loads project's studies list (from project.studies blob or /api/rob/projects/:projectId/studies if available). No special flag required.
behaviors.howToTest: Navigate to /app/project/:projectId?tab=extraction. Assert: tab content loads. Studies table renders with rows for each study in the project. If no studies, 'No studies' message shown.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction add new study
behaviors.description: User clicks 'Add Study' button. Modal opens (or inline form). User fills in study metadata (author, year, title, outcomes). Click 'Save'. New study added to project.studies and persisted via autosave.
behaviors.howToTest: In ExtractionTab, click 'Add Study'. Modal opens with form fields (author, year, title, etc.). Fill in: author='Smith', year='2020', title='Test Study'. Click 'Save'. Assert: study appears in table. Reload page. Assert: study persists.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction edit study
behaviors.description: User clicks a study row. Study editor opens (inline card or modal). User edits fields (outcomes, effect sizes, sample sizes, etc.) and saves. Changes persist to project blob via autosave.
behaviors.howToTest: Click a study in the table. Assert: editor form opens with current study data. Change author field. Click 'Save'. Assert: table updates. Reload. Assert: change persists.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction delete study
behaviors.description: User clicks 'Delete' on a study. Confirmation dialog. Click 'Delete'. Study removed from project.studies. Persisted via autosave.
behaviors.howToTest: Click a study's delete button. Confirm deletion. Assert: study removed from table immediately. Reload. Assert: study is gone.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction continuous effect-size calculator (SMD, MD)
behaviors.description: User selects calculator type 'SMD' or 'MD'. Fills in 6 fields (Mean Exp, SD Exp, n Exp, Mean Ctrl, SD Ctrl, n Ctrl). Clicks 'Calculate & Apply'. Effect size computed via calcES(), displayed + saved to study.
behaviors.howToTest: In study editor, select ES type 'SMD'. Fill: meanExp=10, sdExp=2, nExp=50, meanCtrl=8, sdCtrl=2.5, nCtrl=50. Click 'Calculate & Apply'. Assert: ES value + CI displayed. Assert: study fields (es, lo, hi) update.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction 2x2 dichotomous (OR/RR/RD)
behaviors.description: User selects OR, RR, or RD. Fills 4 cells (a, b, c, d: event/exp, no-event/exp, event/ctrl, no-event/ctrl). Validates: all cells numeric, non-negative integers, no double-zero for OR/RR. Applies continuity correction if zero cell detected.
behaviors.howToTest: Select ES type 'OR'. Fill: a=5, b=45, c=8, d=42. Click 'Calculate & Apply'. Assert: OR computed, result shown. Change to RR: should work. Then set a=0, c=0. Click 'Calculate & Apply'. Assert: error 'Both event cells are zero — use RD instead'.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction zero-cell continuity correction
behaviors.description: When calculating OR or RR with any cell=0, Haldane–Anscombe 0.5 correction automatically applied. User sees warning: 'Zero event cell detected — 0.5 continuity correction (Haldane–Anscombe) applied.'
behaviors.howToTest: Select OR. Fill: a=0, b=100, c=5, d=95. Click 'Calculate & Apply'. Assert: correction applied (result uses log(a+0.5) instead of log(a)). Warning message displayed: 'Zero event cell...'.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction invalid-data validation
behaviors.description: User enters invalid data (non-numeric, negative, missing fields). Calculator shows error before computing: 'Enter all four 2x2 cells', 'Counts must be numbers', 'Counts must be non-negative integers'.
behaviors.howToTest: Try: (1) leave a blank → error 'Enter all four'. (2) enter 'abc' in a → error 'Counts must be numbers'. (3) enter -5 in a → error 'non-negative integers'.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction save + autosave
behaviors.description: User edits study fields or calculator results. Changes autosave to project blob (via updateProject(activeId, patch)). On reload, all changes persist.
behaviors.howToTest: Edit a study field (e.g., change outcome name). Wait 2s (autosave debounce). Reload page. Assert: change persists. Check localStorage or project API call: project.studies should contain updated study.

behaviors.name: Data Extraction export
behaviors.description: User clicks 'Export'. Data exported as CSV, Excel, RIS, JSON, etc. (from openExportDialog). Downloaded file contains studies + extracted data.
behaviors.howToTest: Click 'Export' button. Select format 'CSV'. File downloads. Open CSV. Assert: header row (author, year, n, es, lo, hi, etc.) + data rows for each study.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis page load (flag ON)
behaviors.description: When networkMetaAnalysis=true, workspace NmaTab renders. Shows measure selector, reference input, model selector, 'Run' button, and dataset builder. Dataset defaults to DEFAULT_NMA (empty or example).
behaviors.howToTest: Set networkMetaAnalysis=true. Navigate to /app/project/:projectId?tab=nma. Assert: tab content loads. Measure selector shows OR/RR/RD/MD options. Reference input visible. 'Run Analysis' button present.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis page load (flag OFF)
behaviors.description: When networkMetaAnalysis=false, NmaTab renders disabled note: 'Network Meta-Analysis is not enabled.' Button/tabs are not present.
behaviors.howToTest: Set networkMetaAnalysis=false. Navigate to /app/project/:projectId?tab=nma. Assert: disabled note text + lock icon visible. No form controls.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis measure + model selection
behaviors.description: User selects effect measure (OR, RR, RD, MD, GENERIC). User selects effect model (random, fixed). Selections saved to project.nma.sm and project.nma.model via updateProject.
behaviors.howToTest: In NmaTab, select 'Risk ratio' from measure dropdown. Select 'Random effects' model. Assert: updateProject fires with {nma: {sm: 'RR', model: 'random', ...}}. Reload page. Assert: selections persist.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis reference treatment
behaviors.description: User enters or selects a reference treatment (baseline). Saved to project.nma.reference. Used in contrast estimation.
behaviors.howToTest: In NmaTab, fill reference treatment field: 'Placebo'. Assert: project.nma.reference = 'Placebo'. Reload. Assert: persists.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis required inputs validation (empty state)
behaviors.description: User tries to run analysis with missing data (no studies, no measure, invalid network geometry). POST /api/nma/run returns 400 + errors. UI shows error toast/banner.
behaviors.howToTest: With empty dataset, click 'Run Analysis'. Assert: POST /api/nma/run returns {ok:false, errors:[{level:'fatal', msg:'No studies provided'}]}. Error message displayed in UI: 'No studies' or similar.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis forest plot rendering
behaviors.description: On successful run, POST /api/nma/run returns forest plot data. Forest plot SVG renders, showing treatment comparisons + CIs. Clicking treatments highlights their node.
behaviors.howToTest: Run NMA with valid example dataset (3+ treatments, 4+ studies). Assert: forest plot SVG renders. Each treatment appears as a row with diamond point estimate + horizontal CI line. Scroll/zoom works.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis P-score ranking
behaviors.description: Results include P-score ranking table. Shows each treatment's rank (1=best) + P-score (0-1). Table sortable by P-score or rank.
behaviors.howToTest: Run NMA. Scroll to P-score section. Assert: table renders with 3+ rows (one per treatment). Column headers: Treatment, Rank, P-score. Values correct (Rank 1-N, P-score 0-1).

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis heterogeneity metrics (I², tau, Q)
behaviors.description: Results show heterogeneity: I² (inconsistency %), tau² (between-study variance), Q statistic + p-value. Warnings if I² high (>75%).
behaviors.howToTest: Run NMA. Scroll to results. Assert: section showing 'Heterogeneity: I² = 65%, τ² = 0.12, Q = 8.3 (p=0.01)'. If I² >75%, warning banner appears.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis inconsistency detection (node-split + global)
behaviors.description: Results include inconsistency analysis: node-split p-values for each comparison + global inconsistency (if applicable). Warnings if p<0.05.
behaviors.howToTest: Run NMA. Scroll to Inconsistency section. Assert: table with columns: Comparison, Direct, Indirect, P-value. If any p<0.05, warning: 'Potential inconsistency detected'.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis empty-data handling
behaviors.description: If dataset has 0 studies or 1 treatment, run returns {ok:false, errors:[...]}. UI shows error: 'At least 2 treatments required' or similar.
behaviors.howToTest: Dataset with 1 treatment. Click 'Run'. Assert: error 'At least 2 treatments required'. Dataset with 0 studies: error 'No studies'.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis insufficient data (too few comparisons)
behaviors.description: Network too sparse (e.g., 5 treatments but no paths connecting them). Run returns {ok:false, errors:[...]}. Error: 'Network is disconnected' or 'Insufficient connectivity'.
behaviors.howToTest: Build dataset: 4 treatments, but studies only connect Arms A-B and C-D (no link between A/B and C/D). Run. Assert: error 'Network is disconnected' or similar.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis data flow from extraction
behaviors.description: NMA reads arm/event data from extraction studies (if project.studies populated with arms). Validate confirms data is in correct format before run. After run, results display.
behaviors.howToTest: In extraction, create studies with arm-level data (treatment, events, n). Switch to NmaTab. Dataset should auto-populate (if backend wires extraction → nma). Run. Assert: forest plot renders with arms from extraction.

behaviors.name: Meta-Analysis export
behaviors.description: Results can be exported (PDF report, JSON data). GET or POST export endpoint. File downloads with analysis summary + plots.
behaviors.howToTest: Run NMA. Click 'Export'. Select 'PDF'. File downloads. Open PDF. Assert: title, treatments, forest plot image, P-score table, heterogeneity stats present.

behaviors.name: RoB + Extraction + NMA tab switching (no state loss)
behaviors.description: User navigates between extraction, rob, nma tabs. Switching tabs does not lose unsaved changes (autosave should flush; if not autosaved, warning shown).
behaviors.howToTest: In ExtractionTab, edit a study field but do NOT save. Click RoBTab. Assert: either (a) 'unsaved changes' warning, or (b) changes were autosaved. Return to ExtractionTab. Assert: study still shows the edit.

In global-setup or per-test: PUT /api/settings (admin). Set featureFlags.rob_engine_v2=true. OR: use request fixture to call POST /api/admin/settings/flags with {rob_engine_v2: true}.

seeding.what: Studies in a project (for extraction/RoB testing)
seeding.how: Manually insert via POST /api/projects/:projectId/studies or seed via database directly. Or: use extraction UI to create studies inline. Studies autosave to project.studies blob.
In seed or test: invite a user to the workspace, grant canAssessRiskOfBias permission via workspace settings API. Log in as that user. Restricted to RoB actions (no extraction, no workspace edit).
POST /api/rob/assessments with projectId + studyId to create. Populate answers via PUT /api/rob/assessments/:id/answers. Finalize via POST /api/rob/assessments/:id/finalise.
Hard-code example dataset in test or via fixture. Use nmaTab.jsx exampleDataset() function (3 treatments, 4 studies, multi-arm trial).
primeStitch() in stitch-test fixture sets localStorage metalab_ui_design=stitch. Every test using test (not anonTest) gets Stitch. Assert html[data-ui-design='stitch'] after page load.
