map.area: search+protocol — Search Wizard, Protocol/PICO, Search Builder, Pecan Search Engine
map.summary: The search+protocol area is a unified research strategy pipeline. It spans from PICO question definition → Search Builder (concept/term/database selection with live MeSH lookup) → Search Wizard (3-step Define/Build/Run) → Pecan Search Engine (multi-database execution with duplicate detection and PRISMA reporting). The Architecture uses two parallel tabs: legacy SearchTab (when searchEngine OFF) and new SearchWizard+SearchBuilder (when searchEngine ON). The Protocol/PICO page supports server-backed state (serverBackedWorkflowState flag) that replaces the legacy in-blob PICOTab with ProtocolModulePanel. Feature flags: searchEngine (toggle Search Builder), pecanSearch (toggle Search & Discovery run; requires searchEngine), serverBackedWorkflowState (toggle Protocol module). Autosave is debounced; live MeSH/PubMed counts refresh on term changes. Runs are polled every 2.5s; realtime SSE pokes trigger refetch.

routes.notes: Main workspace container; tabs are dispatched via selector. Tab switching via URL hash or click.
PICO/Protocol tab. Routes to ProtocolModulePanel (server-backed, when serverBackedWorkflowState=true) or legacy PICOTab (when false).
Search tab. Routes to SearchWizard (3-step when searchEngine=true) or legacy SearchTab (when false).
PROSPERO protocol generator (server-backed module).
GET: load saved strategy. PUT: save strategy (autosaved). Wired to SearchBuilderTab, SearchWizard.
POST { term } → { mesh, meshUI, synonyms, ... }. Live dropdown in SearchBuilderTab.
POST { term } → [{ mesh, ... }]. Typeahead suggestions.
POST { query } → { count }. Live hit-count badge in SearchBuilderTab.
Returns [{ id, label, platform, available, selectable, defaultCap, maxCap, ... }]. Seed source cards in PecanSearchTab.
POST { canonicalQuery } → { ok, errors, warnings }. Pre-flight check.
POST { canonicalQuery, sources, overrides } → { translations }. Per-source translated queries.
POST { canonicalQuery, sources, overrides } → { counts }. Estimated results per source (Build step).
GET: list past runs (paginated). POST: start new run (Idempotency-Key required). Returns { run, created }.
Get active run summary + state (completed|failed|cancelled|partial|in_progress).
Paginated list of ambiguous record pairs (hit+count fields). Seed DuplicateReview.
POST { action: 'merge'|'keep_separate'|'defer' }
PRISMA-S structured report.
Download query param ?format=json|csv|html
Returns { featureFlags: { searchEngine, pecanSearch, serverBackedWorkflowState, ... } }

featureFlags.howToEnable: AdminConsole → Settings → Feature Flags → toggle Search Builder Engine ON (server-side only)
AdminConsole → Settings → Feature Flags → toggle Pecan Search Engine ON. Requires searchEngine=true (dependency enforced client-side in pecanSearchFlagEnabled).
AdminConsole → Settings → Feature Flags → toggle Server-Backed Workflow State ON. Replaces legacy in-blob Protocol/PICO with server-backed ProtocolModulePanel.
AdminConsole → Settings → Feature Flags. AI-powered screening assist (used in Protocol/PICO for refining question, deriving PICO from question, suggesting eligibility criteria).

uiElements.name: KeywordField — concept token buttons (click to select)
uiElements.bestSelector: getByRole('button', { name: /click to select|click to unselect/ })

uiElements.name: SearchBuilderTab — MeSH search dropdown
uiElements.bestSelector: [data-testid='mesh-dropdown'] or the typeahead menu rendered on focus

uiElements.name: SearchBuilderTab — StepNav (Define/Build/Run steps in the header)
LimitsPanel — pub type toggles (RCT, Review, etc.)
PecanSearchTab — Live progress card (status, hit count, ETA)
CriteriaList — inclusion/exclusion criterion rows
uiElements.bestSelector: getByPlaceholderText(/Adults ≥18 with confirmed T2DM|Add a criterion/)

behaviors.name: SearchWizard — step navigation
behaviors.description: User can click Define/Build/Run step pip buttons to navigate. Completed steps show checkmark. User cannot reach Run unless builder has concepts. Step transitions persist live in-memory query.
behaviors.howToTest: Open SearchWizard. Click Run pip without concepts → no-op (disabled). Add concept. Click Run pip → advances to step 3. Click Build pip → returns to step 2 with builder still mounted, preserving edits.

behaviors.name: SearchBuilderTab — live MeSH lookup
behaviors.description: As user types in term field, dropdown queries NLM via /api/search-builder/mesh-suggest. Results show mesh label + UI code + synonyms. Selected term populates vocab object (mesh/emtree/scope). MeSH unavailable falls back to CORE_VOCAB seeds.
behaviors.howToTest: In SearchBuilderTab, type 'type 2 diabetes' in a new concept term field. Dropdown appears with MeSH suggestions. Select 'Diabetes Mellitus, Type 2' → term.vocab becomes {mesh: 'Diabetes Mellitus, Type 2', meshUI: 'D003924', ...}.

behaviors.name: SearchBuilderTab — live PubMed hit count
behaviors.description: When user changes concepts/terms, a debounced POST to /api/search-builder/count queries PubMed with the synthesized query. Response updates the live hit badge (e.g. '1,234 hits'). Stale queries are ignored. Failures show '—'.
behaviors.howToTest: In Build step, add concept with term. After 700ms debounce, hit count badge appears with number. Edit term → count refreshes. Clear terms → count resets.

behaviors.name: SearchBuilderTab — form validation (required concepts)
behaviors.description: buildWarnings() surfaces errors (no terms) and warnings (empty P/I/O fields, zero hits, >100k hits). Build step shows warning cards. User cannot advance to Run unless at least one concept has a term.
behaviors.howToTest: In Define step, leave all fields empty. Go to Build step → shows 'No search terms yet' error. Add one concept with one term → error disappears.

behaviors.name: SearchBuilderTab — autosave to /api/search-builder/:projectId
behaviors.description: Every change (add concept, edit term, toggle database, set limit) triggers a debounced PUT /api/search-builder/:projectId with full strategy state. Debounce is 800ms. Failures are silent (best-effort). On page reload, fresh strategy loads via GET, preserving edits.
behaviors.howToTest: Add concept + term in SearchBuilderTab. Refresh page. Strategy persists. Check Network tab → PUT /api/search-builder/:projectId batches multiple field changes.

behaviors.name: SearchWizard — BuildEstimates card (preview counts per source)
behaviors.description: When user enters Build step, BuildEstimates can POST /api/pecan-search/projects/:projectId/preview-count with live query. Returns { counts: { [provider]: {count, kind} } }. Loads show '*' for estimates. If pecanSearch flag OFF, shows 'enable in Ops' note.
behaviors.howToTest: In Build step with searchEngine=ON and pecanSearch=OFF: BuildEstimates shows disabled note. With both ON, click 'Estimate results' → counts populate for each selected database.

behaviors.name: PecanSearchTab — source selection and cap editing
behaviors.description: User toggles checkboxes to select sources (PubMed, Europe PMC, etc.). Each source card shows availability + translation warning. User can edit per-source override query or cap. Overrides are debounced-saved in local state, not persisted to server until Run is started.
behaviors.howToTest: In PecanSearchTab Run step, uncheck a source → source card dims. Edit a source's query override → field updates but Run state unchanged. Edit cap number → updates locally. Start Run → override + cap sent to server.

behaviors.name: PecanSearchTab — Run creation with Idempotency-Key
behaviors.description: User enters run name (optional) and clicks Start. Client generates UUID idempotency key and POSTs /api/pecan-search/projects/:projectId/runs { name, canonicalQuery, sources, caps } with Idempotency-Key header. 202 Created or 200 if replay. run summary returned + stored in state. Live polling begins at 2.5s interval.
behaviors.howToTest: Start a run. Click Start again immediately → same run is returned (202 vs 200 in Network tab, but UI shows no duplicate). Browser tab reload → run.id is polled, resuming live status.

behaviors.name: PecanSearchTab — live progress polling (ACTIVE_POLL_MS=2500)
behaviors.description: While run.state is not in TERMINAL set (completed|failed|cancelled|partial), client polls GET /api/pecan-search/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId every 2.5s. Realtime SSE poke on channel 'search:runId' triggers immediate refetch (not trusted as data). Progress card updates with live hit count, state, ETA.
behaviors.howToTest: Start a run. Watch Live Progress card update every 2.5s. Open server logs → observe search queue. Cancel run → progress state shows 'cancelled'. Refresh page → polling resumes from last known runId.

behaviors.name: PecanSearchTab — duplicate review (paginated)
behaviors.description: When run completes, Duplicate Review section loads paginated list of ambiguous record pairs (score, matchType, reasons). User clicks Merge/Keep Separate/Defer. POST to /api/pecan-search/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId/duplicates/:decisionId/resolve { action }. Page reloads on submit.
behaviors.howToTest: Complete a run that returns deduplication candidates. Scroll to Duplicate Review. Click 'Merge' on first pair → POST fires, pair resolves. Page reloads with next set.

behaviors.name: PecanSearchTab — completion report and export
behaviors.description: When run completes, Completion card shows final counts + links. User can export report via /api/pecan-search/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId/report/export?format=json|csv|html (browser download). PRISMA-S sections visible in HTML.
behaviors.howToTest: Complete run. Scroll to Completion card. Click Export JSON → file downloads. View in browser → contains hit counts, source breakdown, dedupe summary, links to records.

behaviors.name: PecanSearchTab — history and safe retry
behaviors.description: History section shows past runs (paginated, 10 per page). Each row is clickable to load that run's summary. User can Retry an old run (POST /api/pecan-search/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId/retry) — returns new run with same query but fresh execution.
behaviors.howToTest: After a few runs, scroll to Search History. Click a past run row → details load. Click Retry → new run.id returned with state 'in_progress'. Old run remains in history.

behaviors.name: PICODispatcher — feature flag routing (serverBackedWorkflowState)
behaviors.description: On mount, PICODispatcher checks workflowStateFlagEnabled(). If true, renders ProtocolModulePanel (server-backed module with revision conflict detection). If false, renders legacy PICOTab. onMirror callback syncs module edits back to project.pico for other tabs.
behaviors.howToTest: AdminConsole toggle serverBackedWorkflowState ON. Refresh Protocol tab → module loads with distinct styling. Toggle OFF → legacy PICOTab renders.

behaviors.name: SearchWizardDispatcher — feature flag routing (searchEngine + pecanSearch)
behaviors.description: On mount, SearchWizardDispatcher checks searchEngineFlagEnabled() and pecanSearchFlagEnabled(). If searchEngine=OFF, renders legacy SearchTab. If searchEngine=ON & pecanSearch=OFF, renders SearchWizard with Run step disabled (shows 'enable in Ops' note). If both ON, full wizard.
behaviors.howToTest: AdminConsole: searchEngine OFF → legacy SearchTab. searchEngine ON, pecanSearch OFF → wizard steps 1–2 work, run step shows 'Search & Discovery' disabled note. Both ON → full wizard.

behaviors.name: PICOTab — PICO field collaborative locks
behaviors.description: useFieldLock hook monitors presence of other users editing pico.P/I/C/O. If another user holds lock, field is disabled (opacity 0.6, cursor not-allowed), with 'User X is editing' badge.
behaviors.howToTest: Open Protocol tab in two browser windows as different users. In window A, focus P field. In window B, P field disables with lock badge. Window A blurs → lock releases, window B enables.

behaviors.name: PICOTab — AI button (Refine Question, Split PICO, Suggest Criteria)
behaviors.description: AI buttons call aiService.callClaude with structured prompts. Refine Question → refined question text. Split PICO → parses JSON or fallback line parsing, fills P/I/C/O. Suggest Criteria → parses ## sections, converts bullets to • format. Disabled if AI_FEATURES_ENABLED=false.
behaviors.howToTest: Enter a research question. Click 'Refine Question' → AI rewrites it (if enabled). Click 'Split into PICO' → P/I/C/O autofill. Edit PICO fields, click 'Suggest Criteria' → inclusion/exclusion bullets appear.

behaviors.name: CriteriaList — add/edit/remove criterion rows
behaviors.description: CriteriaList parses/serialises to string '• item\n• item'. Add button appends empty row. Edit fires onChange with updated string. Remove button deletes row. String is serialised back, so legacy screening keyword extraction still works.
behaviors.howToTest: Edit inclusion criteria. Add criterion. Edit text. Remove criterion. Refresh page → all edits persisted. Check protocol.pico.incl in network tab → string format preserved.

behaviors.name: SearchBuilderTab — keyword selection from PICO text
behaviors.description: KeywordField displays PICO.keywords as clickable tokens. Click selects/deselects for that PICO field. Manual input adds custom keyword. Selected keywords populate the concept terms in the builder.
behaviors.howToTest: In Define step, PICO keywords appear (from Protocol tab). Click a keyword → it becomes a term in the current concept. Type custom keyword in the 'Add' input, press Enter → added to concept.

behaviors.name: SearchBuilderTab — database selection and access tiers
behaviors.description: DatabaseCatalogView shows grouped databases (PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, etc.) with access tier badges (free, subscription, mixed). Checkboxes toggle selection. Selected count badge shown. Selection saved in strategy.databases (used by wizard to seed provider selection).
behaviors.howToTest: In Build step, open Database picker. Uncheck Cochrane. Check PubMed + Europe PMC. Count badge updates. Continue to Run → source cards pre-select those providers (if they're available in Pecan).

behaviors.name: Empty state — no concepts
behaviors.description: When SearchBuilderTab has no concepts, the Concept Blocks Bar shows 'No concepts with terms yet — add keywords first.' The PubMed hit count badge is hidden. Run step is disabled.
behaviors.howToTest: Open SearchWizard, go to Define step. No concepts yet → bar shows placeholder text. Add a concept → bar renders first block.

behaviors.name: Empty state — no saved strategy
behaviors.description: When PecanSearchTab loads and no strategy exists (loadCanonicalQuery returns null), queryState='empty' and tab renders 'No search strategy yet — build one in the Search Builder first.'
behaviors.howToTest: Create new project, go to Search tab without building strategy. Runs empty state displayed.

behaviors.name: Empty state — no past runs
behaviors.description: When PecanSearchTab history loads with 0 runs, History section shows 'No search runs yet. Start your first run to see results here.'
behaviors.howToTest: New project, fresh Search tab. History section empty message visible.

behaviors.name: Error state — MeSH lookup backend down
behaviors.description: If /api/search-builder/mesh POST fails, SearchBuilderTab falls back to CORE_VOCAB (small seed list). Dropdown still works, suggestions limited. No error banner (graceful degradation).
behaviors.howToTest: Block NLM endpoint in DevTools. Type 'hypertension' → dropdown shows CORE_VOCAB seeds (heart failure, stroke, etc.). Network tab shows failed POST, but UI continues.

behaviors.name: Error state — PubMed count query fails
behaviors.description: If /api/search-builder/count POST fails, hit-count badge shows '—' (em-dash). No error toast (silent retry on next change).
behaviors.howToTest: Block /api/search-builder/count. Edit a term → hit badge becomes '—'. Unblock → next change re-queries.

behaviors.name: Error state — Run start fails (HTTP 400, 409, 500)
behaviors.description: startRun() catches HTTP errors and surfaces error message in state.startError. Toast or inline banner displays error (e.g. 'Validation failed: concept 'X' is empty'). User can fix and retry.
behaviors.howToTest: Manually POST /api/pecan-search/projects/:projectId/runs with invalid canonicalQuery (missing concept). Error message displays in Start section.

behaviors.name: Persistence — search strategy autosave after edit
behaviors.description: Every change to concept/term/database/limit in SearchBuilderTab triggers debounced saveSearch(projectId, state) (800ms). PUT /api/search-builder/:projectId. On page reload, loadSearch() refetches and rebuilds state.
behaviors.howToTest: Edit strategy (add concept, change term, select database). Watch Network tab → PUT fires after 800ms. Refresh page → same strategy loads. Network tab shows GET /api/search-builder/:projectId.

behaviors.name: Persistence — PICO fields autosave
behaviors.description: PICOTab (legacy) and ProtocolModulePanel (server-backed) use the app's serverStorage autosave mechanism. Changes to pico.P/I/C/O/incl/excl/keywords fire debounced PUT /api/projects/:projectId. Page reload preserves all fields.
behaviors.howToTest: Edit PICO question or P/I/C/O field. Refresh page immediately (before autosave completes) → serverStorage completes pending save. Reload page again → edits persisted.

behaviors.name: Responsive behavior — run status updates mid-operation
behaviors.description: While run is in_progress, Live Progress card is highlighted (border color, background). Hit count and source breakdown update every 2.5s poll. Cancel button is visible + enabled. When state becomes terminal, polling stops, final counts lock.
behaviors.howToTest: Start a real search run. Watch Live Progress card pulse every 2.5s with new data. Source counts increment as they complete. State badge changes from 'In Progress' to 'Completed'. Cancel button disappears.

seeding.what: Create a project with PICO and keywords filled
seeding.how: POST /api/projects { name, pico: { P, I, C, O, keywords } }. Or: visit /workspace/:projectId#protocol, fill PICO fields manually (triggers autosave).

POST /api/search-builder/:projectId { concepts: [{ label, terms: [{text, type, vocab}] }], databases: ['pubmed'], filters: {} }. Or: visit /workspace/:projectId#search, build in UI (triggers autosave).
POST /api/pecan-search/projects/:projectId/runs { name, canonicalQuery: { concepts, filters }, sources: [{ provider: 'pubmed' }], caps: { pubmed: 2000 } } with Idempotency-Key header.

seeding.what: Mock a completed run with dedup candidates
seeding.how: For E2E, either wait for real run to complete (30s–5m typical) or mock API response in test fixture. Duplicate Review paginated list endpoint accepts skip/take params.
POST /api/settings/admin { featureFlags: { searchEngine: true, pecanSearch: true, serverBackedWorkflowState: true } }. Or: AdminConsole UI toggles (login as admin).

gaps: No data-testid on most UI elements — many selectors rely on aria-labels/placeholders (brittle if copy changes). Recommend adding testids to wizard steps, run buttons, source toggles, duplicate action buttons.
MeSH dropdown is rendered dynamically in a portal; may need getByRole queries rather than getByTestId. Typeahead focus/blur logic not fully instrumented for E2E.
Live polling (2.5s interval) is tied to setTimeout; tests may need to mock clock (vitest.useFakeTimers) or wait for flaky timing. Realtime SSE poke is not E2E-testable without a real server.
Debounced saves (800ms SearchBuilderTab, 700ms PecanSearchTab preview) require explicit waits (waitFor with timeout > 800ms).
Duplicate resolution UI re-fetches history on submit; no explicit loading state. Tests must wait for next pageload.
pecanSearch flag co-dependency (requires searchEngine) is enforced client-side in pecanSearchFlagEnabled(). Server-side gate in runService also enforces it, but test fixtures may not mirror both.
Admin feature-flag toggles in AdminConsole save asynchronously (no immediate feedback); tests need to wait for save toast or re-check /api/settings/public.

Stitch UI (admin-only) has separate router; SearchWizard appears in both legacy Workspace (#search tab) and Stitch. Stitch styling may differ (distinct theme tokens). No Stitch-specific E2E guidance yet.
PROSPERO tab (Plan & Protocol) is a separate feature module; not fully covered in this map. Protocol export + PRISMA report generation are server-side only.
serverBackedWorkflowState flag gates both Protocol module AND Search Builder revision sync; conflict detection not E2E-tested. Manual conflict simulation needed.

map.summary: The Beta Waitlist (flag betaWaitlist, isolated DB) gates public signup with a 4-step form (email → questionnaire + country + consent). The Invites system (POST /api/invites/:token/accept) is tied to the ScreenProject membership model with token-based single-use links; notifications track invites via type=PROJECT_INVITE and are polled by a bell component. The Stitch admin UI shows waitlist applicants with status/notes management; all three subsystems (waitlist, invites, notifications) are independently seeded via their server APIs and tested through both public pages (/invite/:token, /) and authenticated views (InvitationsView, NotificationsBell).

routes.notes: When betaWaitlist flag ON: shows BetaWaitlistPage (src/frontend/pages/waitlist/BetaWaitlistPage.jsx) to unauthenticated visitors; otherwise shows Landing. Signed-in users always see Landing.
Preview route (always renders regardless of flag, marked noindex); allows admins to preview the page safely.
Public invite landing; deliberately unwrapped (works signed-in AND signed-out). Shows invite details, provides accept button (if signed-in) or register/login links (if signed-out).
Stitch dashboard home; contains StitchDashboard which includes StitchProfileLink → InvitationsView for invite listing.
Stitch project workspace; target of successful invite acceptance.

featureFlags.howToEnable: Via admin console or direct DB update; controls whether BetaWaitlistGate shows the waitlist page on / to unauthenticated visitors. When ON, the public homepage becomes the waitlist form; when OFF, shows the regular Landing page.

behaviors.name: Submit Waitlist Application
behaviors.description: User fills email, country, consent, and optional questionnaire fields (7 questions), submits; receives 201 with success message and honest confirmation state (no fabricated queue position).
behaviors.howToTest: POST /api/waitlist with valid payload; verify response { ok: true, duplicate: false, status: 'WAITLISTED', emailStatus: 'pending|sent|failed' }. Confirm UI shows success modal with correct email-delivery line.

behaviors.name: Duplicate Email Detection
behaviors.description: User submits with email already on waitlist; server returns 200 with duplicate:true; UI shows 'already on the list' message WITHOUT revealing the submission status.
behaviors.howToTest: Submit twice with same email; second attempt returns { ok: true, duplicate: true, message: 'This email is already on the PecanRev beta waitlist' }. Verify UI does not leak status/count details.

behaviors.name: Email Validation (Client & Server)
behaviors.description: Invalid email format rejected inline and on submit; server 422 { errors: { email: 'Enter a valid email address.' } }.
behaviors.howToTest: Submit with 'notanemail' → client-side error; press through honeypot field and invalid email → server 422 error map routes to email field with focus.

behaviors.name: Country Required Validation
behaviors.description: Country field is required (GDPR); omitting it returns 422 with 'Select your country.' and routes focus to country select on retry.
behaviors.howToTest: Fill email + consent but leave country empty; submit returns 422. UI focuses country field, shows error text. Back to earlier step then retry works.

behaviors.name: Consent Required (Two Layers)
behaviors.description: Operational consent (required, explicit true) must be checked; research consent is optional. Server enforces consent !== true → 422 { errors: { consent: 'You must agree...' } }.
behaviors.howToTest: Fill all fields but leave operational consent unchecked; submit returns 422. Research consent is optional → unchecked is ok when operational is checked.

behaviors.name: Custom Role Conditional Validation
behaviors.description: When role='Other' is selected, customRole field becomes required; omitting it returns 422 { errors: { customRole: 'Tell us your role.' } }.
behaviors.howToTest: Select role='Other', leave customRole empty, submit → 422. Fill customRole, submit → success.

behaviors.name: Team Size Conditional Validation
behaviors.description: When workingStyle='Research team', teamSize becomes required.
behaviors.howToTest: Select workingStyle='Research team', leave teamSize empty, submit → 422. Fill teamSize, submit → success.

behaviors.name: Honeypot Anti-Bot
behaviors.description: Hidden website field; if populated, submission returns benign 201 success WITHOUT writing to DB (no log, no email).
behaviors.howToTest: Programmatically fill website field in POST body; verify 201 { ok: true, duplicate: false } but no DB row created.

behaviors.name: Resend Confirmation Email
behaviors.description: User can request re-send of confirmation email via POST /api/waitlist/resend; response is anti-enumeration (same message whether email exists or not).
behaviors.howToTest: Call resendWaitlist(validEmail) → 200 { ok: true, message: 'If that email is on the waitlist, we have re-sent...' }. Call with invalid email → same response. Rate-limited: second attempt within cooldown → 429.

behaviors.name: Rate Limiting (Email Resend)
behaviors.description: Resend endpoint rate-limited per IP; second request in rapid succession returns 429 { error: 'Please wait a moment before requesting another email.' }.
behaviors.howToTest: Call resendWaitlist twice in quick succession; second returns 429.

behaviors.name: Waitlist Count (Public API)
behaviors.description: GET /api/waitlist/count returns { count: N } or { count: null } when unavailable; response always 200 (no 5xx exposed).
behaviors.howToTest: Fetch /api/waitlist/count; verify 200 with count field. Card on homepage shows count or hides gracefully if null.

behaviors.name: Get Invite Details (Public, Pre-Auth)
behaviors.description: GET /api/invites/:token returns sanitized landing page info (projectName, inviterName, roleLabel, masked email, expiresAt) for valid, non-expired, pending tokens.
behaviors.howToTest: Create invite token (via API seeding), fetch GET /api/invites/:token → 200 { projectName, inviterName, roleLabel, email: 'j***@e***.com', expiresAt }.

behaviors.name: Invite Invalid/Revoked State
behaviors.description: GET /api/invites/:token returns 404 for invalid, revoked, or already-accepted tokens (no distinction to prevent enumeration).
behaviors.howToTest: Fetch with non-existent token → 404 { error: 'This invite is invalid or no longer available' }. Create and accept an invite, fetch again → 404 (token nulled).

behaviors.name: Invite Expired State
behaviors.description: GET /api/invites/:token returns 410 for expired tokens (inviteExpiresAt < now).
behaviors.howToTest: Create invite with expiresAt in past; fetch → 410 { error: 'This invite has expired' }.

behaviors.name: Accept Invite (Signed-In)
behaviors.description: POST /api/invites/:token/accept with auth requires valid token; nulls inviteTokenHash (single-use), sets userId + status='active' + inviteAcceptedAt, emits realtime poke, triggers notification.
behaviors.howToTest: Create invite for user@example.com, sign in as that user, POST accept → 200 { projectId, projectName }. Fetch token again → 404 (single-use).

behaviors.name: Accept Invite (Already Member)
behaviors.description: User already has a member row in the project (invited under different email); accept consumes the token (nulls hash) and returns success pointing to the project (no duplicate member row created).
behaviors.howToTest: Add user as member with email A, create invite for email B, sign in as user, accept invite for email B → 200 { projectId }. Verify only one member row exists.

behaviors.name: Accept Invite (Signed-Out → Create Account)
behaviors.description: Signed-out user clicks invite link, sees accept page with 'Create account' button, navigates to /register?invite=<token>, creates account, accept flow completes automatically or via explicit accept on /invite/:token.
behaviors.howToTest: Create invite, load /invite/:token unauthenticated → shows invite details + 'Create account' button. Click → /register?invite=<token>. Register → redirects to /invite/:token (login adapter); accept succeeds.

behaviors.name: Accept Invite (Signed-Out → Sign In)
behaviors.description: Signed-out user on /invite/:token, clicks 'Sign in', signs in, returns to /invite/:token with accept button visible.
behaviors.howToTest: Load /invite/:token unsigned out → 'Sign in' button. Click → /login?invite=<token>. Sign in → redirects to /invite/:token; accept button now visible.

behaviors.name: List Notifications (Authenticated)
behaviors.description: GET /api/notifications (requireAuth) returns newest-first list, unread count, total count; supports ?limit=, ?page=, ?unread=1 (unread only), ?all=1 (include dismissed).
behaviors.howToTest: Fetch GET /api/notifications → 200 { notifications: [...], total: N, unreadCount: M }. Fetch with ?all=1 → includes dismissed rows. Filter works: ?unread=1 → only readAt=null && dismissedAt=null.

behaviors.name: Get Unread Count (Polled by Bell)
behaviors.description: GET /api/notifications/unread-count returns { count: N } for unread notifications (readAt=null && dismissedAt=null); polled every 30s by bell, or every 120s if SSE healthy.
behaviors.howToTest: Fetch GET /api/notifications/unread-count → 200 { count: N }. Create notification, fetch again → count incremented.

behaviors.name: Mark Notification Read
behaviors.description: POST /api/notifications/:id/read sets readAt=now (idempotent); 404 on foreign ids (existence hiding).
behaviors.howToTest: Create notification, POST read → 200 { notification: {..., readAt: ISO } }. POST read again (idempotent) → same notification. POST with wrong user's id → 404.

behaviors.name: Dismiss Notification
behaviors.description: POST /api/notifications/:id/dismiss sets dismissedAt=now (idempotent); dismissed rows hide from active list unless ?all=1.
behaviors.howToTest: Create notification, POST dismiss → 200 { notification: {..., dismissedAt: ISO } }. Fetch list → row absent (dismissedAt != null). Fetch with ?all=1 → row present, dimmed.

behaviors.name: Click-Through Notification (Opened)
behaviors.description: POST /api/notifications/:id/opened stamps readAt + dismissedAt + clickedAt in one idempotent call; bell optimistically removes row from active list and decrements badge immediately.
behaviors.howToTest: Create unread notification, POST opened → 200 { notification: {..., readAt, dismissedAt, clickedAt } }. Bell shows row removed and badge decremented. Call again → idempotent, same timestamps.

behaviors.name: Mark All Notifications Read
behaviors.description: POST /api/notifications/mark-all-read sets readAt=now on every unread row (where readAt=null); returns { updated: N }.
behaviors.howToTest: Create 3 unread notifications, POST mark-all-read → 200 { updated: 3 }. Fetch list → all have readAt set. Badge shows 0.

behaviors.name: Bell Poll with SSE Realtime
behaviors.description: When SSE stream (notification.created poke) is healthy, bell stretches poll to 120s (pokes do the work); on SSE failure, reverts to 30s fallback. Pause polling when tab hidden.
behaviors.howToTest: Open bell, listen to SSE 'notification.created' poke; verify badge updates immediately. Disconnect SSE → bell falls back to 30s poll. Hide tab → no polls; unhide → resumes.

behaviors.name: Bell Deep-Linking (PROJECT_INVITE)
behaviors.description: Clicking notification with relatedMetaLabProjectId deep-links to /app/project/<id> (?tab=screening if relatedScreenProjectId set); clicking with only relatedScreenProjectId goes to /sift-beta/projects/<id>.
behaviors.howToTest: Create invite notification with relatedMetaLabProjectId, click row → navigates to /app/project/<id>. Create with both ids → /app/project/<id>?tab=screening.

behaviors.name: Notifications Mark Opened on Navigation
behaviors.description: Opening a notification calls opened() (read+dismiss+clicked), awaited for full-reload navigations (/app → /app?project=...), concurrent for SPA navigations.
behaviors.howToTest: Create notification, click → opened() called before navigation. Same-path navigations block on opened() (short timeout) to avoid unload aborting request.

behaviors.name: Show History Toggle in Bell
behaviors.description: Bell footer toggle between 'Show history' (active unread/undismissed only) and 'Back to active' (include dismissed); refetch list with ?all=1 when toggled.
behaviors.howToTest: Dismiss a notification, click 'Show history' → list refetches with ?all=1, dismissed row reappears dimmed. Click 'Back to active' → dismissed rows hidden again.

behaviors.name: InvitationsView Dashboard (Stitch)
behaviors.description: Stitch /app shows InvitationsView with pending invitations (Notification.type=PROJECT_INVITE, readAt=null, dismissedAt=null) and earlier invitations (readAt or dismissedAt set). Opening an invite marks it opened and deep-links to project.
behaviors.howToTest: Create invitation notification, navigate to /app → InvitationsView shows pending. Click → notification marked opened, navigates to project.

behaviors.name: Persistence: Waitlist Data Isolated
behaviors.description: Waitlist data (applicants, confirmations) stored in separate DB (BETA_WAITLIST_DATABASE_URL) completely isolated from main user DB; no user account created on submission.
behaviors.howToTest: Submit waitlist application, verify no user row created in main DB. Check waitlist DB directly (via admin API or configured target) → applicant row exists.

behaviors.name: Persistence: Invite Single-Use
behaviors.description: After accept, inviteTokenHash nulled; token is permanently consumed; token can never be reused.
behaviors.howToTest: Create and accept invite, attempt accept with same token → 404 (not found). Verify DB row has inviteTokenHash=null and inviteAcceptedAt set.

behaviors.name: Persistence: Notification State
behaviors.description: Notification state persisted: readAt, dismissedAt, clickedAt are permanent per user; SSE poke triggers real-time refresh of badge + list.
behaviors.howToTest: Create notification, read it, refresh page → still marked read. Dismiss, refresh → dismissed. Verify DB row has readAt/dismissedAt timestamps.

seeding.how: PATCH /api/settings/features (admin only) with { betaWaitlist: true }, or direct DB update. On next anonymous page load, BetaWaitlistGate shows waitlist page on /.

POST /api/waitlist with complete payload { email, countryCode, consent: true, ...optional questionnaire fields }. Response: { ok: true, duplicate: false, status: 'WAITLISTED', emailStatus: 'pending|sent' }.

seeding.what: Create screening project + invite member by email
seeding.how: POST /api/screening/projects with { title }, then POST /api/screening/projects/:id/members with { email, role, inviteExpiresAt }. Returns ScreenProjectMember with inviteTokenHash and generates Notification.type=PROJECT_INVITE.

seeding.what: Register user and navigate to invite link
seeding.how: POST /api/auth/register with email matching invite, then navigate to /invite/:token. Page loads with accept button visible. POST /api/invites/:token/accept → 200, navigate to /app/project/:id.
POST invite (creates PROJECT_INVITE notification), then POST /api/notifications/:id/dismiss. Bell list refetch shows dismissed row absent (unless ?all=1).
POST invites for multiple projects (each creates notification), verify /api/notifications/unread-count returns count > 1. Bell badge shows count.
With browser connected to /app, submit waitlist or create invite via API in parallel tab. SSE stream emits 'notification.created'; bell badge updates immediately without polling.

seeding.what: Admin: list and filter waitlist applicants
seeding.how: GET /api/admin/beta-waitlist/applicants (requireAdmin) with query params: ?status=WAITLISTED, ?role=Faculty, ?countryCode=US, ?search=keyword. Returns paginated rows with metrics.
PATCH /api/admin/beta-waitlist/applicants/:id/status with { status: 'INVITED', note: 'approved for wave 1' }. Triggers audit log.

seeding.what: Admin: resend confirmation email to applicant
seeding.how: POST /api/admin/beta-waitlist/applicants/:id/resend with optional { force: true }. Returns { applicant, emailConfigured }.

gaps: No data-testid attributes on custom form fields (TextField, SelectField, RadioGroupField, CheckboxGroupField); tests must rely on id= or aria-label. Add testid to high-value elements (role select, country select, questionnaire radios, consent checkbox).
NotificationsBell uses inline styles and close-click detection; no stable aria-label or role for the panel itself. Add data-testid='notification-panel' to the panel div for reliable targeting.
InvitationsView Row lacks unique identifier (no data-testid per row); tests must use text matching or nth-child. Add testid with applicant email or id for stability.
Waitlist confirmation email delivery mocked in test mode (no actual SMTP); verify emailStatus in response but cannot assert real email sent without E2E mail service mock.
Invite token expiry (inviteExpiresAt) tested by time comparison; no clock mocking helper. Tests may need to fast-forward date or create tokens with past expiresAt to test 410 expired state.
SSE realtime poke (notification.created) requires an open WebSocket connection; tests must not interrupt the connection mid-test. Monitor frame=user-message events for poke arrival.

Bell polling cadence changes based on SSE health; tests cannot directly inspect the poll timer. Verify indirectly: create notification, wait ~30s unsealed, badge updates. With SSE healthy, cadence stretches to 120s (not easily testable in isolated E2E).

Waitlist admin API (GET /api/admin/beta-waitlist/metrics, PATCH applicant status) requires admin role and waitlist DB configured; graceful degradation (configured: false) when DB unavailable needs integration test with DB down.
Custom consent copy version (CONSENT_VERSION in betaWaitlist.js) bumped on legal changes; tests do not verify version stamped on record. Add assertion: applicant.consentVersion === '2026-06-26'.

map.area: FILES & PDF VIEWER (AppPdfViewer, pdfjs-dist, file upload, PDF viewer controls)
map.summary: The PDF viewer system (AppPdfViewer.jsx + pdfSearch.js) is a universal in-app viewer used across three main workflows: (1) Screening tab — preview/upload/manage article PDFs with open-access auto-find; (2) RoB (Risk-of-Bias) workspace — displays the same screening PDF in a side panel; (3) Data Extraction — not yet integrated but backend upload/download ready. File uploads are handled server-side (screeningPdfController.js) with 25MB limit, magic-byte validation, and Range-request support for streaming. The viewer uses pdf.js with a virtualized continuous-scroll render (IntersectionObserver + ResizeObserver), HiDPI canvas backing, real text-layer search with match highlighting, and full zoom/rotate/navigation controls. Both legacy and Stitch UIs render the same components; Stitch admin tests run via seeded project + authentication.

routes.notes: Middle column tab in legacy screening UI. PdfViewer loads the screening record's PDF attachment, allows upload/replace/remove for members with canScreen role, open-access auto-find available. Query param tab=screening selects this tab.

RoB/extraction workspace. RobPdfPanel wraps the same PdfViewer to show the screening record's PDF (if the study was handed off from screening). Displays in a side panel with flush=true (fills panel height edge-to-edge). Records without screening handoff show empty state.
Backend routes: POST upload (multipart/form-data field=file), GET metadata list, GET/:aid/download (streamed, Range-aware), DELETE/:aid. All require getProjectAccess (member only) + audit logging.

uiElements.name: Find open-access PDF button
uiElements.bestSelector: text=Find open-access PDF or getByRole('button', {name: /find open-access/i})

uiElements.name: Fit width button (fixed-width zoom label, e.g. 'Fit width' or '100%')
Scroll container (virtualized page list, role=group parent)
Open in new tab link (error state)

featureFlags.default: true (can be disabled by admin in MetaSift settings)
featureFlags.howToEnable: Server-side setting in screening/settings.js. When false, POST /api/projects/:pid/records/:rid/pdf returns 403 'PDF upload is currently disabled by the administrator'. Tested in screeningPdfController.pdfUploadMiddleware().

behaviors.name: File upload success (valid PDF)
behaviors.description: User picks a PDF file via input[type=file accept=pdf]; client validates MIME type + .pdf extension; sends multipart POST to /api/projects/:pid/records/:rid/pdf. Server verifies magic bytes (first 5 bytes = %PDF-), size <= 25MB, and file type. On success: 201 response, attachment metadata returned, file written to server/storage/screening-pdfs/:projectId/:uuid.pdf, PdfViewer reloads attachment list and opens preview.
behaviors.howToTest: In a seeded screening project: call test.fileUploadPdf(projectId, recordId, samplePdfPath); assert response 201; navigate to ScreeningTab; assert PdfViewer shows filename + size; assert Preview button enabled.

behaviors.name: File upload rejection (invalid file type)
behaviors.description: User picks a non-PDF file (e.g. .txt, .jpg) or a PDF with wrong MIME type. Client-side validation rejects before upload, or server multer rejects with 400 'Only PDF files are allowed'. Server also checks magic bytes, so a file with .pdf extension but non-PDF content is rejected 400 'File is not a valid PDF'.
behaviors.howToTest: Attempt to upload a .txt file or a JPG renamed to .pdf. Assert error toast message: 'Only PDF files are accepted' (client) or 'File is not a valid PDF' (server). Assert attachment not created.

behaviors.name: File upload rejection (size > 25MB)
behaviors.description: User attempts to upload a file larger than 25MB. Client-side HTML5 input doesn't enforce, but server multer.limits.fileSize = 25MB will reject with 413 or 400 error. screeningPdfController detects err.code === LIMIT_FILE_SIZE and returns 400 'PDF exceeds the 25MB limit'.
behaviors.howToTest: Generate a 26MB valid PDF; attempt upload. Assert 400 error with message about size limit. Assert no attachment created.

behaviors.name: PDF viewer opens + renders first page sharp
behaviors.description: After successful upload, PdfViewer opens (click Preview or open=true by default). AppPdfViewer fetches the PDF from GET /api/projects/:pid/records/:rid/pdf/:attachmentId/download. Browser's Range request support is advertised by the server (Accept-Ranges: bytes). The first page dimensions are measured up-front (Task 5), the viewer calculates fit-to-width scale, and the canvas renders at HiDPI backing-store scale (dpr), making text crisp. Pages are virtualized: only pages near the viewport are rendered via IntersectionObserver + renderRange.
behaviors.howToTest: In ScreeningTab, after uploading a multi-page PDF, assert: (1) Preview button is visible and clickable. (2) Click Preview → AppPdfViewer appears in-app (not iframe). (3) First page is rendered without blur/fuzz. (4) Page indicator shows '1 / N'. (5) Toolbar buttons (zoom, rotate, search, next) are enabled.

behaviors.name: PDF zoom in (ladder stepping)
behaviors.description: Click zoom-in button or Ctrl/⌘ + wheel up. AppPdfViewer.applyZoom() uses a fixed ZOOM_LADDER [0.25, 0.5, ..., 5] to step up predictably. Current effective scale (fit-width derived or userScale) is clamped to [SCALE_MIN=0.25, SCALE_MAX=5]. Each click moves to next rung. Content point under viewport center (or cursor for wheel) is captured (anchor) before zoom, then restored after re-render, keeping the page stable under the zoom point.
behaviors.howToTest: Open a multi-page PDF in fit-width mode. Click zoom-in 5 times. Assert: (1) Page grows. (2) Zoom label changes from 'Fit width' to '100%', '125%', etc. (3) Scroll position moves to keep the page center visible. (4) Zoom-in button disables at 500% (SCALE_MAX).

behaviors.name: PDF zoom out (ladder stepping)
behaviors.description: Click zoom-out button or Ctrl/⌘ + wheel down. Same ladder mechanism as zoom-in, but downward. Content anchor is captured to keep the visible area pinned.
behaviors.howToTest: From 500%, click zoom-out repeatedly. Assert label decreases (200%, 175%, ...). Assert pages shrink. Assert zoom-out button disables at 25% (SCALE_MIN).

behaviors.name: PDF fit-to-width reset
behaviors.description: Click the zoom-label button ('Fit width' or the percentage). If in custom scale mode, resets userScale = null, re-derives fit-to-width scale from measured container width + first page dims, and re-renders all visible pages. Anchor is captured to keep the viewport stable.
behaviors.howToTest: Zoom to 150%, then click the zoom button. Assert: (1) Zoom label returns to 'Fit width'. (2) Page shrinks back to container width. (3) Pages are responsive to window resize while fit-width is active.

behaviors.name: PDF page navigation (prev/next buttons)
behaviors.description: Click Previous Page or Next Page (or arrow keys: ArrowLeft/PageUp = prev, ArrowRight/PageDown = next). scrollToPage(n) sets programmaticScroll timestamp, updates pageNum, mounts pages n-1..n+1 immediately, then smoothly scrolls to page top (scrollTo { behavior: smooth }). The scroll handler fires, recomputeWindow() updates renderRange, and the indicator shows the current (most-visible) page.
behaviors.howToTest: Open a 10-page PDF. Assert page indicator = '1 / 10'. Click Next 3 times. Assert indicator = '4 / 10' and page 4 is visible. Click Prev. Assert indicator = '3 / 10'. Use arrow keys: press ArrowRight → Next fires. Press PageUp → Prev fires.

behaviors.name: PDF page indicator accuracy
behaviors.description: The indicator (e.g. '5 / 10') shows the most-visible page, determined by pageAtOffset(scrollTop + viewportHeight / 2). Updated only after user scrolls (not after programmatic scrolls for 250ms guard). Never shows a blank page because pages are mounted before scroll navigation and the scroll window extends into the render buffer.
behaviors.howToTest: Open a PDF. Scroll manually to different pages. Assert indicator tracks the visibly-centered page. Programmatically call scrollToPage(7). Assert indicator updates to 7 but without the scroll-handler delay (smooth scroll masks it).

behaviors.name: PDF rotation (left 90°, right 90°)
behaviors.description: Click Rotate left/right buttons. rotation state rotates ±90° (modulo 360). displayDims() swaps width/height for rotations 90/270. All pages re-render at the new rotation. Content anchor is captured to keep the viewport center stable (captureAnchor(null) for center, which reads viewport mid-point).
behaviors.howToTest: Open a portrait PDF. Click Rotate right. Assert: (1) Page is now landscape. (2) Scroll position adjusts to keep the center visible. (3) Click 4 times → returns to 0°. (4) Text layer rotated (data-main-rotation CSS attribute applied).

behaviors.name: Live search across all pages (no Enter, as-you-type)
behaviors.description: Click Search button → search panel opens. Type in input. After 160ms debounce, AppPdfViewer scans all pages (1..numPages) for matches using findMatchesInText(), building a flat list of {page, local} match offsets. Each page's text is fetched (cached) from pdf.js getTextContent(). Returns flat ordered matches and scrolls to first match. As user types more, new scan supersedes old (via scanToken). Match case / whole-word toggles affect the regex flags.
behaviors.howToTest: Open a PDF with 'diabetes' on pages 2, 5, 8. Click Search. Type 'diabetes'. Assert: (1) No Enter needed. (2) After 160ms, status shows 'X / Y' matches. (3) Page scrolls to page 2 automatically. (4) Clicking Next match scrolls to page 5, 8 in order. (5) Toggle 'Match case' → no new results if PDF is all-lowercase. (6) Toggle 'Whole words' → 'diabetic' no longer matches.

behaviors.name: Search highlighting with match cursor
behaviors.description: Each text span in the rendered text layer is scanned for matches. Found text is wrapped in <mark> elements (background: HL color). The currentLocal match gets an extra 'cur' class (different background + box-shadow outline). On match navigation (prev/next via cycleMatch), currentLocal changes, mark elements update, and curEl.scrollIntoView({block: center}) brings the current match into the viewport within the page.
behaviors.howToTest: Search for 'the' in a PDF. Assert: (1) Multiple <mark> elements on the visible page. (2) One <mark> has class='cur' (orange/darker color). (3) Click Next match → a different <mark> gets class='cur'. (4) Page scrolls to keep the current match visible. (5) Type a new search term → old marks removed, new ones applied.

behaviors.name: Search state reset on close or ESC
behaviors.description: Press Escape in the search input or click Close Search (X button). closeSearch() sets searchOpen=false, clears term/matches/matchIdx, increments scanToken (cancels any in-flight scan). Search panel closes; viewer returns to full height.
behaviors.howToTest: Start a search. Press Escape. Assert: (1) Search panel closes. (2) Marks disappear from pages. (3) Search input is empty. (4) type a new search → new scan starts.

behaviors.name: PDF error: 401/403 (auth failed)
behaviors.description: GET /api/projects/:pid/records/:rid/pdf/:aid/download returns 401 or 403 if user lost access or session expired. AppPdfViewer shows error 'You are not signed in, or you do not have access to this PDF.' with 'Retry' and 'Open in new tab' buttons.
behaviors.howToTest: Upload a PDF to a project. Logout. In another tab, try to view the project's ScreeningTab. AppPdfViewer shows auth error. Click Retry (or manually log back in) → PDF loads.

behaviors.name: PDF error: 404 (missing on disk)
behaviors.description: GET returns 404 if the attachment metadata exists but the file is missing from disk. AppPdfViewer shows 'Could not load PDF' error. 'Open in new tab' still appears (externalUrl = download URL), allowing user to diagnose (browser devtools shows 404).
behaviors.howToTest: Upload a PDF. Manually delete the file from server/storage/screening-pdfs/:projectId/:uuid.pdf. Reload the viewer. Assert 404 error from download handler. AppPdfViewer shows error.

behaviors.name: PDF error: corrupted or non-PDF content
behaviors.description: GET returns 200 but content doesn't start with %PDF- magic bytes, or pdf.js fails to parse. AppPdfViewer detects isPdfBytes(buf) returns false, or task.promise rejects with parse error. Shows 'Could not load PDF — the file may be corrupted or not a valid PDF.'
behaviors.howToTest: Manually edit a stored PDF on disk to corrupt the header. Reload viewer. Assert error message. Click Retry → same error. Deleting the corrupted file then re-uploading works.

behaviors.name: PDF error: network timeout
behaviors.description: fetch() for the download URL times out or rejects with TypeError (network unreachable). AppPdfViewer catches and shows 'Could not reach the PDF (network error). Check your connection and retry.'
behaviors.howToTest: Pull the network cable or mock fetch to throw. Attempt to open PDF. Assert network error message. Restore connection, click Retry.

behaviors.name: PDF viewer staying inside layout (no overlap, flush mode)
behaviors.description: In RoB/extraction workflows, RobPdfPanel wraps PdfViewer with flush=true. AppPdfViewer then uses: display: flex, flexDirection: column, height: 100%, minWidth: 0, flex: '1 1 0%', background: C.card2. The toolbar shrinks (flexShrink: 0), and the scroll area grows (flex: 1, minHeight: 0) to fill remaining height. In normal (non-flush) mode, the viewer has a fixed previewHeight and doesn't grow. No CSS position: fixed or absolute that would break out of parent layout.
behaviors.howToTest: Open RoB workspace. Assert RobPdfPanel (bordered box) contains the PdfViewer. Drag the panel border to resize. Assert: (1) Toolbar stays at top, doesn't overflow. (2) Pages scroll within the box, don't spill over. (3) Page canvas stays inside the scroll container (ResizeObserver re-fits on resize).

behaviors.name: PDF no blank page on arrival (virtualization)
behaviors.description: When navigating to a page via scrollToPage(n), renderRange is pre-mounted to [n-1, n+1] BEFORE the smooth scroll starts. By the time the user sees the page, it is already rendered (not blank). IntersectionObserver is not used for page mounting (too async); instead, renderRange is computed deterministically from scrollTop + viewport bounds and the cumulative pageTops array.
behaviors.howToTest: Open a 20-page PDF. Click Next repeatedly, watching carefully. Assert: (1) No blank pages between turns. (2) Page indicators are always correct. (3) Slow network: artificially delay canvas render, then scroll. Assert page number is still correct (blank canvas, but page is mounted).

behaviors.name: Keyboard navigation (arrow keys + PageUp/PageDown)
behaviors.description: onKeyDown(e) in AppPdfViewer fires for ArrowLeft/PageUp → prev(), ArrowRight/PageDown → next(). Only active when the viewer has focus (tabIndex=0 on the shell). If user is typing in a text input, target.tagName = INPUT and the handler returns early (doesn't scroll).
behaviors.howToTest: Click the PDF viewer to focus it. Press ArrowRight → Next fires. Press PageDown → Next fires. Press ArrowLeft → Prev fires. Click search input, press ArrowRight → no page nav (search input is active). Press Escape to close search, then ArrowRight → works again.

behaviors.name: Ctrl/Cmd + wheel zoom (smooth pinch)
behaviors.description: A non-passive wheel listener on the scroll container. If Ctrl or Cmd is held, preventDefault() stops the browser zoom, then applyZoom(s * 1.1 or s / 1.1) zooms smoothly around the cursor position (anchorClientY = e.clientY). No discrete ladder stepping; continuous scale. After releasing, the next zoom button click picks up from that exact scale.
behaviors.howToTest: Open PDF. Hover near a text block. Hold Ctrl (Cmd on Mac) and scroll up. Assert: (1) Text zooms toward cursor, not viewport center. (2) Smooth animation, not steps. (3) After scrolling, click zoom-in button. Continues from the current scale.

behaviors.name: Replace PDF (delete old, upload new)
behaviors.description: In PdfViewer, when canManage=true and attachment exists, a 'Replace' button appears. Click → file input. Pick a new PDF → onPick() validates type + size, uploads to same endpoint, which first deletes the old attachment+file (screeningPdfController.uploadPdf deletes existing before creating new), writes the new file, creates new metadata row. Response has new attachment ID. PdfViewer reloads attachment list and opens preview.
behaviors.howToTest: In ScreeningTab as a member: upload PDF v1. Click Preview to confirm. Click Replace → pick PDF v2. Assert: (1) Confirmation that new file is uploaded. (2) Preview shows v2 (check title/content). (3) Old file deleted from disk. (4) Attachment ID changed.

behaviors.name: Remove PDF (delete attachment + file)
behaviors.description: In PdfViewer, if canManage=true and attachment exists, 'Remove' button is present. Click → confirms via deletePdf() API call. Server deletes the screenPdfAttachment row and the stored file. PdfViewer clears attachment state, hides preview.
behaviors.howToTest: In ScreeningTab: upload and view a PDF. Click Remove. Assert: (1) Confirmation message. (2) PDF disappears. (3) 'Upload PDF' button returns. (4) File deleted from disk.

behaviors.name: Open-access auto-find (experimental, feature flagged)
behaviors.description: In PdfViewer, if canManage=true and no attachment, a 'Find open-access PDF' button is shown. Calls screeningApi.oaRetrieve(pid, [recordId]). On success with status='attached', reloads attachment and opens preview. On 'failed', shows actionable state with retry/open-source-link/manual-upload. On 'skipped_no_doi' or 'rate_limited', shows specific message.
behaviors.howToTest: In ScreeningTab, for a record with a DOI but no PDF: click 'Find open-access PDF'. (Test requires OA service backend to be configured.) Assert: (1) Button shows 'Searching…'. (2) On success, preview opens. (3) On failure with sourceUrl, user can 'Open source link ↗' or 'Upload PDF manually'.

behaviors.name: Permission enforcement: non-members cannot upload
behaviors.description: In PdfViewer, if canManage=false, the 'Upload PDF', 'Replace', 'Remove' buttons are hidden. The 'Find open-access PDF' button is also hidden. Server-side, screeningPdfController.uploadPdf checks access.canScreen && access.isLeader → 403. getProjectAccess() enforces membership.
behaviors.howToTest: Invite a viewer-role member to a screening project. As viewer, open ScreeningTab. Assert: (1) No upload/replace/remove buttons. (2) Can see preview (if attachment exists). (3) Try to POST /api/.../pdf directly → 403 'You do not have permission'.

behaviors.name: Permission enforcement: only uploader or leader can delete
behaviors.description: Server-side, deletePdf() checks att.uploadedBy === req.user.id || access.isLeader. If neither, returns 403 'You cannot remove this attachment'.
behaviors.howToTest: User A uploads a PDF. User B (member, not leader) sees the PDF. User B clicks Remove → 403 error shown. User A (uploader) can remove. Project leader can also remove.

seeding.how: POST /api/screening/projects { title: 'E2E Test Project' }. Returns {id, title}. Authenticated user becomes 'owner'. Required before uploading a PDF.
POST /api/screening/projects/:projectId/records { title: 'Sample Article', year: '2024' }. Returns {id, title, year}. Required before attaching a PDF.

POST /api/projects/:projectId/records/:recordId/pdf (multipart form-data field='file'). Send valid PDF bytes (sample PDF can be generated with pdfkit, or use a fixture PDF file). Returns 201 {attachment: {id, recordId, fileName, fileSize, mimeType, createdAt}}.
GET /api/projects/:projectId/records/:recordId/pdf. Returns {attachments: [{id, recordId, fileName, fileSize, ...}]}. Used to verify upload succeeded.

GET /api/projects/:projectId/records/:recordId/pdf/:attachmentId/download (with credentials: 'include'). Returns PDF bytes, Content-Type: application/pdf, Content-Disposition: inline, Accept-Ranges: bytes. AppPdfViewer uses this URL.

Generate a multi-page PDF with known content (e.g. 'Page 1 text', 'Page 2 text', 'diabetes mentioned here') using pdfkit or similar, save to e2e/fixtures/sample.pdf. Use in test: page.locator('input[type=file]').setInputFiles('e2e/fixtures/sample.pdf'); or call uploadPdf helper.

seeding.what: Add project members for permission testing
seeding.how: POST /api/screening/projects/:projectId/members { email: 'user@test.local', role: 'reviewer' }. Returns {member: {id, email, role, status}}. Used to test canManage=true/false for upload/replace/remove.

gaps: No data-testid attributes exist in AppPdfViewer or PdfViewer components. All selectors rely on getByRole/getByLabel/getByPlaceholder, which are brittle if aria-label or button labels change. Recommendation: add data-testid to all toolbar buttons, search input, page indicator, and scroll container.

ResizeObserver and IntersectionObserver are not mocked in E2E tests; they work fine in real browser but tests relying on virtual scroll or page reflow may race (e.g., canvas renders async in pdf.js worker). Monitor for flakiness on slow CI.

PDF.js worker is bundled as a .mjs file (pdfjs-dist/legacy/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs?worker). Vite proxy must serve with correct MIME type. If MIME type is wrong, the worker fails silently and falls back to fake worker, causing rendering to hang. This is already fixed in the code (see AppPdfViewer line 37) but not tested in E2E.

Large PDF handling (100+ pages): the viewer uses virtualization, but tests should validate that rendering doesn't stall, and that search across 100+ pages completes in reasonable time. No explicit timeout test exists.

Range request support: screeningPdfController.downloadPdf advertises Accept-Ranges and handles range headers. Browser PDF viewer (chrome especially) uses this. E2E tests don't explicitly validate Range requests; network mocking should verify the 206 response for partial content.

Session cookie riding on download request: AppPdfViewer fetch() uses credentials: withCredentials ? 'include' : 'same-origin'. Tests must ensure cookies are included; anonTest contexts should fail 401. Not explicitly tested.

Text layer search resilience: if a page's pdf.js getTextContent() throws, the page is skipped (catch returns empty items). Tests don't verify this graceful degradation; should add a corrupted/broken-parse PDF test.

Anchor capture and scroll restoration: ResizeObserver and the zoom/rotate anchor logic are complex and timing-sensitive. Manual testing is more thorough than E2E for this; consider adding playwright.debug() on flaky zoom tests.
Admin feature flag allowPdfUpload: there is no E2E test for disabling PDF uploads via admin settings. Should add a test that sets flag=false and verifies 403 on upload.
Open-access auto-find: backend integration is not present in the test environment unless OA service is mocked. Tests skip this or use a feature-flag mock.
No test for corrupted PDF on disk (file exists but unreadable). Should mock fs.readFileSync to throw EACCES and verify error handling.
Keyboard navigation test should verify that search input focus blocks arrow keys, and that tabbing into the viewer enables arrow keys.

map.summary: PecanRev has a dual role hierarchy: (1) **Global User Roles** (user, admin, mod, unauthenticated) stored on User.role, enforced by middleware (requireAuth, requireAdmin, requireRole); and (2) **Screening Project Roles** (owner, leader, reviewer, viewer) with 8 permission presets on ScreenProjectMember, scoped by project access checks (getProjectAccess). Routes are gated by middleware stacking (e.g., /api/admin/* → requireAuth → requireAdmin), and project-scoped operations branch on isOwner/isLeader/canManageMembers flags. Roles are ALWAYS verified from DB, never trusted from JWT; session revocation is enforced via sessionEpoch + suspended flag on every requireAuth call. Feature flags (aiScreening, rob_engine_v2, etc.) default OFF and must be checked at the client to hide/disable UI.

behaviors.name: Authentication flow
behaviors.description: User registers → verifies email → logs in → receives httpOnly session cookie. Password reset via token link (no admin intervention needed).
behaviors.howToTest: Call POST /api/auth/register with email + password; verify 200 response. Call POST /api/auth/verify-email with token. Call POST /api/auth/login with credentials; check Set-Cookie header for SESSION_COOKIE.

behaviors.name: Project autosave never fails
behaviors.description: PUT /api/projects/:id/autosave PINNED CONTRACT: always returns 200, never 4xx. Read-only members and outsiders get { id, skipped: true, readOnly: bool, reason: string } instead of 403/404.
behaviors.howToTest: Call PUT /api/projects/:id/autosave as a read-only member on a shared project. Expect 200 { skipped: true }. Verify project is NOT persisted.

behaviors.name: Project soft-delete hides from all lists
behaviors.description: DELETE /api/projects/:id or POST /api/projects/:id/delete sets deletedAt + deletedSource:'owner'. Project hidden from owner's list and returns 404 on direct access. Admin can restore via PATCH /api/admin/projects/:id/restore.
behaviors.howToTest: Owner deletes their project (200 { deleted: true }). Call GET /api/projects; project absent. Call GET /api/projects/:id; 404. Admin calls PATCH /api/admin/projects/:id/restore (200); project reappears.

behaviors.name: Workspace member role enforcement
behaviors.description: PUT /api/projects/:id access enforced per role: owner → always 200; leader/canEditMetaLab → 200 annotated; viewer/read-only → 403. Import/export similarly gated.
behaviors.howToTest: Create project + add workspace member as viewer. Try PUT /api/projects/:id as viewer; expect 403 { error: 'Read-only access...' }. Try as leader; expect 200.

behaviors.name: SSE heartbeat keeps stream alive
behaviors.description: GET /api/events stream sends 'retry: 5000', ':connected' comment, then ':hb' heartbeat every ~25s. Client can detect dead socket via close event.
behaviors.howToTest: Subscribe to GET /api/events as authenticated user. Verify heartbeat comments every ~25s. Disconnect socket; verify 'close' event fires.

behaviors.name: Notifications never over-counted when multiple tabs open
behaviors.description: GET /api/notifications/unread-count always returns caller's global unread count (unread = readAt null AND dismissedAt null). Polling returns same count across tabs.
behaviors.howToTest: Create notification in tab A. Poll /api/notifications/unread-count from tab B; count matches. Mark read in tab A; poll from tab B; count decrement propagates.

behaviors.name: Rate limiting on auth endpoints
behaviors.description: Auth routes (/api/auth/register, /api/auth/login, /api/auth/forgot-password, /api/auth/reset-password) limited to 20 req/15min per IP in production. Dev/test relax to 1000.
behaviors.howToTest: Make 21 POST /api/auth/login requests in <15min from same IP. 21st returns 429.

behaviors.howToTest: Admin enables maintenance mode. Call GET /api/projects as non-staff; expect 503. Call as admin; expect 200. Call /api/health; expect 200.

gaps: Missing explicit request/response bodies for most endpoints in route files (consult controllers/ for actual schema validation). API contract doc (server/docs/api-contract.md) shows expected shapes but some new endpoints (e.g., /api/screening/projects/:pid/ai/*, /api/pecan-search, /api/workspaces) lack detailed specs. Feature flag names/behavior inferred from route guards and controllers; exact conditions where endpoints return 404 vs 503 vs 401 should be verified per handler. Admin-only endpoints lack explicit error responses for insufficient-role cases (consult requireAdmin/requireAdminOrMod middleware + tests). Session cookie name and exact httpOnly/SameSite/Secure flags derivable from server/config/cookies.js (not fully read). Rate limiter configurations for some endpoints (citationLimiter, institutionLimiter, etc.) exist but are not documented in contracts. POST /api/screening/projects/:pid/ai/*, /api/pecan-search/*, /api/workspaces/* routes not fully enumerated (files not exhaustively read). Import/export error handling and retry semantics not fully specified. No explicit documentation of expected JWT claim shape for admin/mod role verification. Soft-delete resurrection guard and cascade semantics complex; exact conditions where DELETE vs POST/:id/delete used need developer confirmation.
map.summary: PecanRev's API surface consists of ~120+ public endpoints organized across auth (register/login/logout/me, password reset), profile (get/update, change password), projects CRUD (with soft-delete and archive), nested studies/records, meta-analysis computation (pooled, sensitivity, subgroup, Egger, trim-fill), validation, import/export (RIS/BibTeX support, durable async jobs), notifications (bell + per-user inbox), invites (public token-based, single-use), realtime SSE events, and two parallel UIs: META·LAB (core review/meta-analysis) and META·SIFT (collaborative screening). Admin endpoints govern users, feature flags (aiScreening, rob_engine_v2, searchEngine, pecanSearch, serverBackedWorkflowState), theme/settings, screening module health, AI intelligence ops, onboarding questions, Risk of Bias (RoB v2), and waitlist (separate isolated DB). Auth is httpOnly session cookie with rate-limiting (20 req/15min for auth endpoints, 30 req/15min for public invites). Maintenance mode gates non-staff traffic (exempt: /health, /version, /settings/public, /auth/*, /admin/*, /events). Autosave PINNED CONTRACT guarantees 200 even for read-only members (returns { skipped: true } instead of 4xx).

map.area: Branding + Feature Flags + Accessibility + Responsive Design
map.summary: PecanRev (user-facing brand throughout UI, meta tags, titles) runs on a dual-stack design system: legacy UI (default) and admin-only Stitch UI (Vivid Enterprise). Feature flags control engines (aiScreening, rob_engine_v2, pecanSearch, networkMetaAnalysis, etc.) toggled via PUT /api/admin/feature-flags. Responsive breakpoints at 1024px (drawer nav), 880–980px (grid reflows). Accessibility enforced via ARIA roles, theme tokens with WCAG AA contrast, and prefers-reduced-motion. Internal identifiers (metalab_* localStorage keys, META·LAB docs) retained for backward compatibility; no user-facing leaks.

behaviors.description: Admin can edit appSettings via Ops; changes surface in landing, contact form, banner
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Ops console, set appName to 'MyReview'. 2. PUT /api/admin/settings. 3. Refresh landing. 4. Verify appName in header.

behaviors.name: Brand color palette applies before first paint (no flash)
behaviors.description: index.html bootstrap injects brand palette as inline --t-* properties before React hydrates; no flash of default indigo
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Set custom brandColor to '#ff0000'. 2. Hard-refresh page. 3. Capture first screenshot < 100ms. 4. Verify accent uses custom color (not default #4f46e5).

behaviors.name: Error boundary in Ops recovers from render crash
behaviors.description: OpsErrorBoundary catches render errors in individual sections; shows recoverable message; other sections unaffected
behaviors.howToTest: 1. Seed malformed data in a response. 2. Navigate to that Ops tab. 3. Assert error message (styled alert). 4. Assert other tabs still functional. 5. Click 'Try again'.

behaviors.name: Public endpoint GET /api/settings/public returns no sensitive data
behaviors.description: /api/settings/public (no auth) returns appSettings, featureFlags, themeSettings; no admin emails, API keys, or sensitive metadata
behaviors.howToTest: 1. anonTest (logged-out). 2. Fetch GET /api/settings/public. 3. Assert 200 OK. 4. Verify no admin credentials or API keys in response.

seeding.how: Global setup (e2e/global-setup.ts) creates admin user + stores auth cookie in .auth/admin.json. Test fixture reads storageState automatically.
Global setup creates project, stores project ID in seed.json. tmpProject fixture creates/deletes throwaway project per test.

seeding.what: Stitch UI active on first paint
seeding.how: primeStitch(page) sets localStorage[metalab_ui_design]='stitch' via addInitScript. gotoStitch(page, path) navigates and polls expectStitch() to assert data-ui-design='stitch'.
Call adminApi.featureFlags.save({rob_engine_v2: true, ...rest}) from test context. Persisted to SiteSetting; returned by GET /api/settings/public.

seeding.what: Custom brand color (for theme token tests)
seeding.how: Call adminApi.theme.save({brandColor: '#ff0000', preset: null}). Server generates palette, stores in SiteSetting; bootstrap injects as inline --t-* before first paint.

Call adminApi.onboarding.create({key: 'q1', prompt: 'Test Q', type: 'text', isRequired: true, isActive: true}). Seed user with pendingOnboarding=['q1']. Navigate to protected route; assert redirects to /onboarding.
Call adminApi.settings.save({appSettings: {maintenanceMode: true, maintenanceMessage: 'Down for updates'}}). Hard-refresh public landing. Assert banner displays.

gaps: No data-testid attributes on interactive elements today — all selectors rely on role=, classes (.stitch-*), inline styles. Should add data-testid throughout AdminConsole, feature flag toggles, and key Stitch components.
Stitch responsive behavior below 880px untested — grid reflow media queries exist but E2E suite does not explicitly validate all breakpoint thresholds (880px, 900px, 980px, 1023px).
Accessibility axe-scan not yet integrated — no automated accessibility testing against WCAG 2.1 AA. Manual contrast ratios validated in tokens.js comments, but no CI gate.
Theme bootstrap timing edge case: if localStorage.metalab_brand is stale and server brand differs, brief flash possible during hydration.
Design mode escape param (?ui=stitch) only works for admins; non-admins forced to legacy silently (not documented in UI).
Mobile drawer nav does not have explicit dismiss-on-link-click logic — drawer may stay open after navigation.
Custom brand color validation: server validates hex format, but client does not prevent invalid input before submission (needs client-side validation UI).
Stitch rail expand-on-hover behavior relies on CSS :hover; no keyboard equivalent (Tab does not expand rail).
Error messages in Ops (ErrorBox, NoticeBox) not announced to screen readers — should wrap in live regions with role=alert.
Pagination component (Ops tables) uses arrow buttons; no role=navigation or aria-label='Pagination' — context unclear for screen readers.
ConfirmModal focus management: on close, focus returns to trigger, but if trigger deleted, focus management undefined (edge case).
